American drama, starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Sasha Alexander and Michael Weatherly(n)

programme.desc: Three similar execution-style killings take place, and the team discovers one of the victims was going through a messy divorce. Starring Michael Weatherly and David McCallum(n)

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Walter learns Cabe's secret about the Baghdad mission and it threatens to tear the team apart. Meanwhile, Paige is shocked when Ralph puts his life in danger to help Scorpion. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong(n)

programme.title: Scorpion

programme.title: NCIS

Guest starring Colin Hanks (Gus Grimly in the Fargo TV series)(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates when Navy Seal Chad McBride's wife receives a package from Riyadh in Saudi Arabia containing her husband's severed head, which Palmer discovers contains very high levels of radiation. programme.desc: The American crime drama returns with the team still reeling from the Department of Defence investigation, with Ziva, McGee and Tony having handed in their badges. Crime drama, guest starring Marina Sirtis(n)

programme.desc: Eric manages to land Lovejoy in hospital with a broken leg, so the incapacitated dealer tasks Lady Jane and Tinker with selling a terracotta Chinese pig to a Chinese banker on his behalf. However, it gets broken and inside they find a valuable medieval Chinese bank note. Ian McShane directs and stars(n)

It isn't long before he's reunited with the remaining Chosen Men for one of the most momentous military engagements of all time - the Battle of Waterloo. Sean Bean, Daragh O'Malley, Hugh Fraser and Cecile Paoli star(n)

During the unexpected interlude, they set about making themselves unpopular with local pub landlord Arthur Pringle. Tim Healy and Jimmy Nail star, with Bryan Pringle(n)

programme.title: Auf Wiedersehen, Pet
programme.desc: Wayne's sexual antics with Pringle's daughter result in the lads being evicted from the Barley Mow, leaving them with no alternative but to camp out in Thornley Manor. However, its owner Ally Fraser remains unaware he is providing them with free board and lodging. Tim Healy and Gary Holton star(n)

Funeral director Warren Bugle directs Mike, Kristin and Breen to Wadsworth Manor where a mysterious role-playing game was conducted the night before Declan's death.(n)

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries

programme.desc: Sam faces a race against time as he attempts to forget his troubles and focus on an investigation into the kidnapping of a man's wife and daughter. As he picks through the details of the crime, he begins to consider who may be motivated to hold the pair hostage - only for Annie to leave herself exposed when she discovers the kidnapper's true identity. Fantasy drama, starring John Simm and Philip Glenister(n)

programme.title: Life on Mars
programme.desc: Gene investigates the shooting of a Ugandan Asian man and jumps to the conclusion that the attack is drug-related when he finds a suspicious bag of powder on the victim. When the substance turns out to be heroin, the policeman becomes determined to keep it off the streets of Manchester - and decides to do whatever it takes to get his way. Fantasy drama, starring Philip Glenister and John Simm(n)

programme.desc: A brother and sister stand accused of murdering their father and stepmother, and pleading for them proves to be the most complex assignment of Kavanagh's entire career. The task is made doubly difficult by the man's insistence on defending himself and a tragedy within his own family - and Julia's decision to leave chambers does not help either. Legal drama, starring John Thaw, Anna Chancellor and Art Malik(n)

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: The sleuth delves into a suspicious death, while loyal sidekick Geoffrey and her put-upon husband Robert become involved in a delicate undercover operation. programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: The sleuth is called upon to mediate in a dispute at a condemned block of flats, and soon discovers the man responsible for their construction has a shady past. Further investigations send the part-time sleuth on a tense race against time as she strives to prevent the cowboy builder from profiting from his shoddy work. Detective drama, starring Patricia Routledge(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: The trail of evidence against Colin Fairfax leads Jack Meadows and Jo Masters to a potentially explosive confrontation with him in a lock-up garage full of petrol cans. Yvonne gets wind of Scott's romantic entanglement with Honey and confides in Steve Hunter her suspicions about his cool reaction to the news of his wife's murder. Serial rapist Alan Kennedy demands an interview with Sam Nixon, claiming he has been receiving death threats(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: The spectre of racial unrest looms over Sun Hill following a number of assaults on the Muslim community, allegedly committed by white youths. Hoping to defuse the situation, Leela persuades her father to tell the police he witnessed an Asian man carry out one of the attacks - and later finds him beaten senseless. As the three officers killed in the explosion are laid to rest, Neil arouses his wife's suspicions by attending Andrea's funeral, while Laura is unconvinced by Gabriel's whiter-than-white demeanour(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Leela urges her hospitalised father to make a statement about his attackers, and is warned her own life is in danger - which only increases her determination to pursue the case to the bitter end. Jo goes on the trail of a violent pimp and offers a local prostitute the chance to become a police informant, while Gabriel shows his compassionate side by protecting a little girl he suspects is being abused. Honey brushes aside Yvonne's concerns about her engagement to Scott, putting it down to jealousy - unaware of Yvonne's budding romance with A&E nurse Nick(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Gina faces the ultimate test of character when she learns she has cervical cancer, but is determined to maintain her hard-as-nails demeanour in public. Leela considers leaving the force when it becomes apparent that the chances of convicting drug dealer Salim Haq are slim, while Jo's investigation into a rival villain uncovers ominous evidence of gang warfare. Elsewhere, Gabriel sets out to prove schoolgirl Gemma is being abused by her stepmother - and finds an ally in Sheelagh(n)

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: The sleuth delves into a suspicious death, while loyal sidekick Geoffrey and her put-upon husband Robert become involved in a delicate undercover operation. programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates

Detective drama, starring Patricia Routledge(n)

programme.title: Cranford
programme.desc: First in a two-part special. The story picks up in August 1844, two years after Miss Matty lost her beloved sister Deborah. Mr Buxton has returned to live quietly in Cranford following the death of his wife, although Miss Matty is concerned about his relationship with his son as neither seem to be coping with their bereavement. The local busybody is also worried about Peggy Bell, who lives in an isolated cottage looking after her inattentive mother and domineering brother, so she hatches a plot to bring these young residents together. However, the surprise arrival of Lady Ludlow's son Lord Septimus leads to a chain of unexpected events concerning the looming railway, and a tragedy at Matty's own house forces her to re-evaluate her opinions on the subject. Judi Dench and Jonathan Pryce star(n)

programme.title: Cranford
programme.desc: Conclusion of the two-part special. The women of Cranford are excited about the imminent visit of Lady Glenmire, until the Hon Mrs Jamieson announces that none of them is of high enough social standing to meet her - so Miss Matty leads the Amazons in snubbing both the newcomer and her hostess. But sociable Lady Glenmire is quick to offer an olive branch by hosting a memorable Halloween party, which results in the forging of new friendships. Mr Buxton is impressed by Edward Bell's ambitions, so takes him on as land agent, only for the new employee to make a disastrous decision that has dire consequences for both himself and his sister Peggy. Meanwhile, Matty is distressed when a surprising discovery about one of her close circle results in a serious falling out among the friends, and when tragedy strikes the town, she fears Cranford may never recover. Judi Dench, Celia Imrie and Jonathan Pryce star(n)

programme.title: Miss Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. An unpopular colonel is found dead in the vicarage of St Mary Mead and investigations reveal no shortage of suspects - including the vicar and members of the victim's family. Christmas edition from 1986, starring Joan Hickson, Paul Eddington and James Hazeldine(n)

programme.title: Miss Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. A newlywed, recently arrived in England from New Zealand, sees disturbing visions of a strangled corpse in the hallway of her dream home. Joan Hickson stars as the amateur detective determined to get to the bottom of the mystery(n)

programme.title: The Casual Vacancy
programme.desc: As the parish council election approaches, the townsfolk are on tenterhooks, awaiting the next post from 'the Ghost of Barry Fairbrother'. Who is behind the internet messages and what will they say next? In an attempt to smooth things over, Samantha throws a dinner party for all the warring factions, but when Howard and Shirley unexpectedly arrive things quickly spiral out of control. Across in the Fields, Terri seems to be over her addiction, giving Krystal hope of a new future for her family. Drama adapted from the novel by JK Rowling, starring Michael Gambon, Keeley Hawes, Abigail Lawrie and Keeley Forsyth(n)

programme.title: The Last Post

programme.title: The White Princess
programme.desc: The York princesses are strategically married off to strengthen the Tudor cause, but further political challenges are on the horizon. When King Henry finds himself back on the battlefield to protect his reign, Lizzie learns that the threats they face may be closer to home. Jodie Comer, Jacob Collins-Levy, Suki Waterhouse and Michelle Fairley star(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The squad reinvestigates the death of a timeshare magnate, previously thought to have committed suicide, when a reformed call girl claims she was with him the night he died and saw someone force their way into his house as she left. It then emerges the deceased's widow has since fallen on hard times after being swindled by her late husband's business partner - who is connected to a notorious criminal. Guest starring Shane Richie, Naomi Bentley and Claire Rushbrook(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Gerry celebrates his birthday at a comedy club, where a controversial act comes under fire from a group of hecklers. Sandra discovers the most vocal member of the gang is the niece of a political activist who went missing 25 years previously after waging a campaign against the comedian. Agreeing to reinvestigate the disappearance, the squad discovers that the activist and her boyfriend had infiltrated an extreme right-wing group with links to the dangerous criminal who murdered Jack's wife. Guest starring Phil Daniels, Joe Absolom and John Henshaw(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Gina warns Amber not to tell a soul about her cancer, but any hopes of keeping her illness secret are dashed when she's prescribed a course of daily radiotherapy. programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents action from the latest Premier League fixtures, including Liverpool v Bournemouth at Anfield and Arsenal v West Ham United. Initially flattered by the attention lavished upon him by his wealthy new friend, the grappler flourishes - but tension soon grows between them when his brother is employed as a coach, a situation that leads to tragedy. Fact-based drama, starring Steve Carell, Channing Tatum and Mark Ruffalo(n)

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: Scotland v France (Kick-off 3.00pm). John Inverdale presents coverage of both teams' penultimate match of the championship, held at Murrayfield. Scotland recorded their first win of the campaign last time out against Italy and have won their last three home games against the French. However, Les Bleus have been in excellent form so far in this season's competition and are looking to move a step closer to completing the Grand Slam. With analysis from Andy Nicol, John Barclay, Thomas Castaignede and Paul O'Connell, and commentary by Andrew Cotter, Chris Paterson and Brian Moore(n)

programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: Footage of wildlife inhabiting underwater kelp forests, including thousands of giant cuttlefish spawning along a restricted area of rocky reef off the south coast of Australia. Males outnumber females 11 to one, which leads to fierce competition. Larger males use brute force to drive off competition, while their smaller rivals use deception by mimicking the appearance of females. The programme also features tiger sharks hunting for green turtles in fields of seagrass and spider crabs trying to avoid predators while they shed their shells(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team marks International Women's Day on the Holnicote Estate in Somerset, where Anita Rani meets Holly Purdley from the Women in Farming group, which aims to reduce the isolation often felt by women who live and work on farms. Matt Baker finds out about as innovative river restoration project that is also home to a new colony of beavers. Plus, Hannah Jackson - aka the Red Shepherdess - looks at whether times have really changed for women in farming(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents from the grounds of Salisbury Cathedral in Wiltshire, where the experts value decorative objects and rarities belonging to the public. An eclectic mix includes one of Napoleon's razors, letters from Enid Blyton and an item of Queen Victoria's underwear, as well as a hand-painted Christmas card from the Second World War and original drawings of Judge Dredd(n)

programme.title: Last Tango in Halifax
programme.desc: There is chaos at the bungalow as work begins to strip out the old kitchen, while Alan's new supermarket job brings an unexpected challenge in the form of Harrison. An overburdened Celia is called upon to travel by bus, while the giraffe continues to torment Gillian, who is pushed to her wits' end by the return of PC Cheryl. Elsewhere, a hungover Judith has an epiphany, Caroline gains an unwanted lodger, and the truth about Ted's last-minute trip comes to light.(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents action from today's Premier League fixtures - Manchester United v Manchester City at Old Trafford and Chelsea v Everton at Stamford Bridge. United were looking to complete a double over their local rivals after winning 2-1 at Etihad Stadium in December, while Carlo Ancelotti was facing his former club for the first time since taking over Everton. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's top-flight goals(n)

programme.title: Shane Williams: Rugby, Concussion and Me
programme.desc: Shane Williams heads on a journey to understand how the world of rugby, from grass roots to the highest levels, is working to reduce concussions in the modern-day game and helping to make the sport safer for future generations. He talks to players past and present, the custodians of the game at World Rugby and leading global medical professionals studying the long-term effects of contact sport on the brain(n)

programme.desc: Daniel and Zara have fun trying to keep a secret, while a determined Bear returns from his break. Al tries to help the owner of his favourite pub, whose life is falling apart(n)

programme.title: A Service of Celebration for Commonwealth Day
programme.desc: In the presence of the Queen and other senior members of the royal family, Petroc Trelawny presents live coverage from Westminster Abbey of a service featuring readings, music and dance. Including performances by Alexandra Burke, Craig David and the Melodian steel band, as well as a reflection by world heavyweight boxing champion Anthony Joshua. Sonali Shah interviews honoured guests and participants(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore rescues two items from a recycling centre in Witley, Surrey with creative duo Josh and Oli coming up with an idea that could make a fantastic profit(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Friends and former scout leaders Anne and Jenny take part in the culinary contest, teaming up with chefs Romy Gill and Akis Petretzikis. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Matt Baker present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: Inside Out London
programme.desc: Hanif Qadir investigates how potential terrorists are slipping through the net, while Chris Jackson discovers how dashcam footage is helping tackle dangerous driving. Plus, Sean Fletcher finds out why a Brixton horse club is riding high(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Chantelle comforts Gray when his office returns his belongings although he cannot face telling Whitney what has happened. Jean accuses Suki of lying about her illness, leaving her family disgusted that she could suggest such a thing. Linda is hurt that Mick doesn't trust her when she finds him searching for stashed alcohol. Jay makes a big decision about his future(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The Department for Work and Pensions is meant to help disabled people get back into work. In this programme, reporter Richard Butchins discovers that the DWP has lost more employment tribunals for disability discrimination than any other employer in Britain. He investigates why the DWP has paid its own employees nearly a million pounds of public money in both tribunal pay-outs and out-of-court settlements(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace preside as six more hopefuls compete in the third week of heats. They are challenged to serve up their signature dish, with the judges' favourite two winning a coveted MasterChef apron and a place in the next round. The remaining four must come up with a dish using three specific ingredients, after which two cooks are sent home - leaving the remaining four to cook two courses for former contestants who have gone on to work in the food industry(n)

programme.title: This Country
programme.desc: Kurtan receives some sad news about a relative and endeavours to make some new memories. Meanwhile, Kerry discovers a passion for reading and joins the vicar's book club, which goes well until another village resident joins. Spoof documentary, starring Daisy May and Charlie Cooper(n)

programme.title: Young, Welsh and Pretty Minted
programme.desc: The programme meets 20-year-old Callum Griffiths, who is one of Europe's up-and-coming suppliers of dog biscuits, and designer Ibby Tarafdar, who spends half of his time jet-setting to meet his famous American clients, and the other half working as a barista. Instagram star Erin Budina is branching out with a YouTube channel that challenges her to do everyday jobs - but will working in a recycling depot prove a step too far for her?(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Eddie Izzard hosts an evening of stand-up at the Apollo in Hammersmith, London, treating the audience to his own brand of stream-of-consciousness humour before introducing routines by The Last Leg star Josh Widdicombe and South African comic Trevor Noah(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: The Dog Ate My Homework
programme.desc: Children's comedy panel show hosted by Iain Stirling, featuring studio-based frolics and slapstick challenges(n)

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Radio host Mark Wright and CBBC vlogger Lauren Layfield enlist expert designers and builders to construct dream dens for lucky youngsters(n)

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Lindsey Russell and Richie Driss celebrate World Book Day by announcing the winners of this year's Blue Peter Book Awards and also reveal who will be crowned the best of the past 20 years as voted for by viewers. Plus, a performance by the cast of Six the Musical(n)

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall heads into the Australian outback to fulfil a lifelong dream of finding the most venomous snake in the world, before hunting for the blue-ringed octopus in Sydney(n)

programme.title: Strange Magic (2015)
programme.desc: A fairy living in an enchanted forest tries to rescue her sister from a monstrous creature. Meanwhile, the magical kingdom is thrown into chaos by a love potion, that results in a series of unexpected romances. Animated fantasy musical, with the voices of Evan Rachel Wood, Alan Cumming and Kristin Chenoweth(n)

programme.title: Kung Fu Panda: Secrets of the Furious Five
programme.desc: Animated semi-sequel to Kung Fu Panda in which the history of the fearless defenders of the Valley of Peace is revealed as they perform thrilling feats of bravery. Dragon warrior Po (Jack Black) faces his greatest challenge - teaching a class of novice kung fu students. With the voices of Dustin Hoffman, David Cross, Carol Kane and Jaycee Chan(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: The natural history documentary follows the work of tough Australian Chris 'Brolga' Barnes, who has dedicated his life to rescuing and raising orphaned kangaroos in the outback near Alice Springs. This two-part film follows Brolga as he expands operations by opening his doors to a whole new bunch of animals, including a trio of camels, a southern hairy-nosed wombat and three emu chicks, as well as building a wildlife hospital(n)

programme.title: Lorraine's Fast, Fresh and Easy Food
programme.desc: Lorraine Pascale shows how to cook a range of straightforward and satisfying dishes while juggling a hectic daily routine, and offers tips on making starters, main courses and desserts that are ideal for sharing, as well as quick family meals(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore how menus at Britain's pub have changed dramatically from the days when only nuts and pork scratchings were available for customers. They re-create dishes that would make any self-respecting publican proud, and also try their hand at making cider the traditional way(n)

programme.title: Sport Relief 2019
programme.desc: Comedy star Jennifer Saunders and Strictly Come Dancing professional Oti Mabuse captain the teams in this charity match in aid of Sport Relief. Clare Balding presents the action as players including sports stars Denise Lewis, Katherine Grainger, Jade Jones and Katherine Merry, actresses Sarah Hadland and Sally Phillips, presenters Vick Hope, Ellie Taylor and Lindsey Russell and Love Island's Montana Brown take to the court(n)

programme.title: The £100K House: Tricks of the Trade
programme.desc: Architects Piers Taylor and Gurmeet Sian help two homeowners in need of life-changing transformations to their existing homes. In Sutton Coldfield, West Midlands, they come to the aid of builder Lee, who added an extension to his suburban semi. However, two years later the ground floor is half built, and his partner Jaimelee and their three children are left living in a building site. Meanwhile, Suzanne, Antonio and toddler Sophia struggle with the awkward layout of their west London flat, giving Gurmeet the challenge of rethinking it(n)

programme.title: Hugh's Wild West
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall goes in search of wildlife in the West Country and meets some of the region's most dedicated nature lovers, beginning in the Wye Valley and the Forest of Dean. He catches minnows in the River Wye, gets an insight into the life of Britain's only aquatic songbird and meets an IT technician who has set up miniature cameras in his garden to record animals(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: The continued work of Australian naturalist Chris 'Brolga' Barnes, who has dedicated his life to raising kangaroos and is now opening his doors to new animals. However, it is not proving easy. The emu chicks are wreaking havoc, the camels are rapidly outgrowing their enclosure and the wombat is proving quite a handful. To help, Brolga is moving some of them to his 80-acre sanctuary - but after a clash with Roger the kangaroo, he has been left on crutches, and all his plans are on hold(n)

programme.title: Greg James' Sport Relief Heroes
programme.desc: A defining feature of Sport Relief is the willingness of celebrities to undertake gruelling challenges in order to inspire the public to donate and get involved with the cause. Greg looks back with celebs including David Walliams, Eddie Izzard, Davina McCall, John Bishop and Zoe Ball, all of whom have put themselves to the test and beyond for the charity, finding out what motivated them(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of the unpaid volunteers who sacrifice their free time to staff more than two hundred RNLI lifeboat stations across the UK. In the final episode, the Royal National Lifeboat Institution volunteers race to rescue two teenagers stuck in mud in Cleethorpes on the Humber estuary before the incoming tide overwhelms them. Elsewhere, a team in Devon help a crew of a yacht shipwrecked on a notorious sand bar, and a routine call to pick up a boat that has broken its moorings becomes a race to save a family of four(n)

programme.title: Wild Cuba: A Caribbean Journey
programme.desc: Cuba's isolation has helped preserve many of its natural riches, creating unspoiled landscapes home to many enchanting animals. In the first of a two-part series, cameraman, director and naturalist Colin Stafford-Johnson explores corners of the country that few outsiders have seen. Amongst the wonders he encounters is the bee hummingbird, the world's tiniest bird, and the spectacle of thousands of crabs migrating en masse. In this authored odyssey, the natural magic of one Earth's most intriguing countries is revealed(n)

programme.title: Great American Railroad Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey to Quebec City, beginning by boarding the train from Nova Scotia to New Brunswick. Along the way he investigates the world's biggest tide at Hopewell Rocks and admires its dramatic rock formations and caves. He also appears to defy gravity on a magnetic hill in a 1965 Pontiac Bonneville, before joining the Elsipogtog First Nation to learn about quilting and traditional dress. He also goes fishing for oysters and helps quarry 150-year-old sandstone(n)

programme.title: Hilary Mantel: Return to Wolf Hall
programme.desc: Profile of author Hilary Mantel in the six months leading up to the publication of The Mirror and the Light, the final novel in her award-winning Tudor trilogy telling the story of the extraordinary rise and sudden fall of Thomas Cromwell. Hilary reveals why she embarked on the writing of the trilogy and how it has changed her life. She also delves into her past and how she grew up with a dark family secret(n)

programme.title: Lost Lives (2019)
programme.desc: A documentary inspired by the book of the same name that records the circumstances of the deaths of every man, woman and child killed during the Troubles. With extracts read by stars of stage and screen including Roma Downey, Liam Neeson, Adrian Dunbar, Michelle Fairley, Ciaran Hinds, Bronagh Gallagher, Brendan Gleeson, Susan Lynch, Stephen Rea and Kenneth Branagh(n)

programme.title: Midnight's Children (2012)
programme.desc: Two babies are born at midnight on the day India becomes independent of British rule in 1947, but are switched at birth. As the two children grow to adulthood, one in wealth the other in poverty, they find their lives are constantly intertwined while the nation around them experiences sweeping social change. Drama based on Salman Rushdie's novel, starring Satya Bhabha and Siddharth(n)

programme.title: Stations of the Cross (2014)
programme.desc: A girl from a strict Catholic family is an outsider in her secular school, and faces constant reproach from her fundamentalist mother. Under the tuition of a misguided priest, she pushes herself to extreme acts of self-sacrifice in her pursuit of spiritual redemption. Drama, starring Lea van Acken and Florian Stetter. In German, French and Latin(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Presenter Helen Skelton and expert garden designer Danny Clarke transform a tiny backyard into a beach garden inspired by the neighbouring Barry coastline(n)

programme.title: The A to Z of the RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.desc: Jennifer Saunders presents a guide to the 105-year history of the horticultural event, while Adam Frost shares his memories of being involved in the show as both a designer and a presenter. Plus, Arit Anderson and Frances Tophill travelling the country meeting people who are preparing to exhibit their creations at this year's show, and a profile of the event's most prolific gold medal winner Sarah Eberle(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team visits the Gwent Levels to find out more about a project celebrating the area's fishing and farming heritage, and preserving them for the future. Matt Baker meets a group of fishermen who are still catching salmon the way it's been done for centuries, Ellie Harrison is on the trail of a star species that's made an amazing comeback to the Levels, and Tom Heap is looking at what's being done to ensure those most in need in the countryside can access the healthiest fresh food(n)

programme.title: Beechgrove Repotted
programme.desc: A visit to two unique allotment initiatives that take growing together to a whole different level. First, Moncrieff Allotments in the middle of Perth, a multicultural allotment that brings a diverse group of people together to garden in the middle of the sometimes flooding, Tay. Next, the programme heads to Tillicoultry Allotments in the shadow of the beautiful Ochil hills, which looks like a series of beautifully interlinked back gardens(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Toxicologist Bryan Fry explores the nature of the Komodo dragon, the largest species of lizard in the world. He uses hi-tech tools to take a close look at the creatures and discovers there is a lot more to them than meets the eye, from hidden venom glands to their true origin. Narrated by Peter Capaldi(n)

programme.title: Lucky Jim (1957)
programme.desc: A hard-drinking, unconventional junior lecturer is frustrated with his stuffy university, but still wants to curry favour with the formidable head of the history department. A family gathering and an academic ceremony provide opportunities to make an impression - but nothing goes to plan. Comedy based on Kingsley Amis's novel, starring Ian Carmichael, Terry-Thomas and Hugh Griffith(n)

programme.title: Lorraine's Fast, Fresh and Easy Food
programme.desc: Lorraine Pascale shows off her patisserie expertise as she demonstrates straightforward ways to make a variety of mouthwatering baked dishes, including strawberry cakes and slow-baked leg of lamb. She also shares her recipe for creating a crusty loaf(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Comfort Food
programme.desc: Dave Myers enjoys a taste of his native county when he and Si King demonstrate recipes using produce from Cumbria, including a supper dish made with Cumberland sausage(n)

programme.title: 633 Squadron (1964)
programme.desc: British airmen embark on a daring mission to destroy a heavily fortified German rocket-fuel plant in Norway, and to ensure success the RAF pilots have to bomb an overhanging cliff to collapse it onto the building. The plan threatens to unravel, however, when a Norwegian freedom fighter plotting a land attack to coincide with the air strike is captured by the Nazis. Second World War drama, starring Cliff Robertson, George Chakiris, Donald Houston and Harry Andrews(n)

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: The Arderns get a taste of running a store in the 1920s, 30s and 40s, considered the golden age of the corner shop due to the plentiful availability of packaged food. However, rationing quickly has a devastating impact on both the shop and the family's day-to-day lives, with carrot lollies and mutton bacon proving less than enticing meals. The Arderns learn how to make ice cream by hand and blend tea, as well as taking delivery of a vintage fruit machine. They also find themselves at the centre of a community party recreating the VE Day celebrations(n)

programme.title: Live Women's International Football
programme.desc: Japan Women v England Women (Kick-off 6.15pm). Eilidh Barbour presents coverage of the latest match in the round-robin competition, held at Red Bull Arena in Harrison, New Jersey, as England continue their defence of the trophy. Phil Neville's side recorded an impressive 3-0 victory over Japan last year to secure the title, but they have not been at their best since the 2019 World Cup concluded and will be looking to get back on track before hosting Euro 2021(n)

programme.title: Russia with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer embarks on a journey across the world's biggest country, taking him from the snow-capped volcanoes of the far east, through some of the remotest parts of the planet, to the great cities of the west, Moscow and St Petersburg(n)

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: Five pairs of competitors journey around the globe, carrying only limited cash and unable to get on a plane or use mobile phones and credit cards. In the first edition, the teams set off from Mexico City, hoping to reach the first of seven checkpoints - Copan Ruinas in Honduras. Each team adopts a different strategy, with married couple Jen and Rob quickly deciding on a route via Belize, but confusion on the Metro leads to a delay(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Investors Sara Davies, Touker Suleyman, Deborah Meaden, Tej Lalvani and Peter Jones return to those infamous hot seats as a new batch of entrepreneurs strive to seal a life-changing deal. Hopefuls include a beauty entrepreneur whose tools have a social as well as practical purpose. Two low-alcohol brewers hope the Dragons will stump up for the ultimate round of investment. Plus, the multi-millionaires try more than a business on for size when a footwear entrepreneur pitches his range of hand-finished shoes. Presented by Evan Davis(n)

programme.title: Dara O Briain - Voice of Reason
programme.desc: A chance to see the comedian Dara O Briain's Voice of Reason live show from 2019, filmed at London's Eventim Apollo. In the show, Dara addresses problems with virtual reality, having too much technology at home, how to deal with missing members of the front row and how to cope with reports of your own demise(n)

programme.title: The Ranganation
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts a topical comedy show, in which a diverse group of 25 members of the public drawn from all walks of life are invited to share their thoughts on the state of modern Britain today. Romesh and his outspoken and passionate focus group will be joined in the studio by a selection of celebrity guests(n)

programme.title: Women's Six Nations Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the fourth round of fixtures, including England v Wales at Twickenham Stoop and Scotland v France at Scotstoun Stadium(n)

programme.title: Up There (2012)
programme.desc: A spirit is given the job of escorting the recently deceased to the afterlife, when all he wants to do is cross over himself. He is partnered with an annoyingly cheerful sidekick for his latest assignment, but they manage to lose the soul in question and face a frantic search through a British seaside town. Comic fantasy, starring Burn Gorman, Kate O'Flynn and Aymen Hamdouchi(n)

programme.title: Valhalla Rising (2009)
programme.desc: A mysterious mute warrior leads a life of slavery under a brutal tribal chieftain, but manages to kill his master and escape. Joining a band of Vikings who have embraced Christianity, he is recruited into a mission to conquer Jerusalem. Historical adventure, starring Mads Mikkelsen, Maarten Stevenson and Jamie Sives(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Tunbridge Wells, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Fraud Squad NHS
programme.desc: The team investigate a wealthy manager who has been taking advantage of the NHS, and the million-pound tax fraudster who claimed to be a spy(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore turns to upcycler Jacqui Joseph to transform a pair of chairs and a laundry basket, while designer-maker Daniel Heath hopes to give a small melamine dining table a stylish update. A pair of French armoires give Sarah herself an exciting new challenge(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Sinead's love of cooking came when grandmother Jean taught her as a little girl. However, as she's travelled around the world her tastes have got more adventurous. Cooking against the clock, they are paired up with chefs Anna Haugh and Mike Reid and produce dishes from around the world on a budget of £5, from squid tempura to pad Thai and Korean chicken wings. Plus, the 10-minute challenge throws up cornflakes and ginger nut biscuits. Hosted by Rylan Clarke-Neal(n)

programme.title: Best Home Cook
programme.desc: The contestants are given just three hours to produce their ultimate children's birthday cake - and as if that were not tough enough, Mary Berry reminds them she knows a thing or two about baking. This is followed by Chris Bavin's challenge to rustle something up from leftovers, and he makes the task twice as hard by stripping back the contents of the larders. The judges then decide who will have to compete in Angela Hartnett's eliminator, by the end of which another contestant will be hanging up their apron. Claudia Winkleman hosts(n)

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn and guests discuss the big political issues of the day(n)

programme.title: Women's Six Nations Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the fourth round of fixtures, including England v Wales at Twickenham Stoop and Scotland v France at Scotstoun Stadium(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Three detached properties in Leicestershire are in the spotlight, featuring a retro family home, a country-style new-build and a house with some seriously modern twists(n)

programme.title: Royal Recipes
programme.desc: Michael Buerk celebrates royal food inspired by the days of India and Empire, as Paul Ainsworth cooks up quail and potato curry, said to be a favourite of Queen Victoria. Plus, historian Dr Annie Gray visits Osborne House on the Isle of Wight where Victoria, Empress of India, showcased her grand passion for the raj, and even hired her own Indian chefs(n)

programme.title: Terry and Mason's Great Food Trip
programme.desc: Broadcaster Terry Wogan and London cabbie Mason McQueen continue their culinary journey around the nation in Scarborough, North Yorkshire. In their quest to find the best local food, they visit a fish and chip cafe with a glamorous history, go on a train ride and attend a tea-dance with more than 30 different varieties of cake for them to sample(n)

programme.title: Red Rock
programme.desc: Keith faces a watery demise while trying to buy Mick's silence, Paudge is reluctant to break good news, and Sean may have an ulterior motive in talking to Irena(n)

programme.title: The Great Interior Design Challenge
programme.desc: The two remaining designers compete for a place in the quarter final, this time transforming two tiny bedrooms in medieval timber-framed houses in Sussex. The pair have very different briefs, with one designer facing the challenge of creating a Scandinavian-inspired scheme with a raised built-in bed. The other must deliver a Batman-inspired fantasy den, complete with bat motif lighting, a secret compartment, and dark grey walls. Presented by Tom Dyckhoff(n)

programme.title: Portillo's State Secrets
programme.desc: Michael Portillo looks at files from the National Archives that relate to traitors and spies, including documents that show a king's role in torture and James Bond's secret wartime origins. He also learns how a double agent's escape at the height of the Cold War was a comedy of errors(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Experts James Lewis and Jonathan Pratt assess items brought to Dover Castle in Kent, while presenter Paul Martin looks at the life of James Bond creator Ian Fleming(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Previous winners Hugh Dennis, Sarah Greene, Nish Kumar and Holly Walsh go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sets off on the first leg of a journey from the Midlands to Rye in East Sussex, beginning by travelling from Warwick to Radley in Oxfordshire(n)

programme.title: Coastal Path
programme.desc: Paul Rose explores the spectacular coastline of southern Cornwall, discovering one of the world's finest stages at the Minack open-air theatre in Porthcurno. He also tries out the high-octane sport of coasteering on the Lizard Peninsula, joins a National Trust ranger for some clifftop wildlife spotting, and fires the Tudor cannons of Pendennis Castle in Falmouth(n)

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites four more contenders to brave the black chair, answering questions on the specialist subjects of the life and written works of Laura Ingalls Wilder, Star Trek: The Original Series, the life and career of Jenson Button, and Mary, Queen of Scots. They then each have two minutes to demonstrate their general knowledge(n)

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell presents the first semi-final of the quiz as two teams compete to guess the connections between things that seem utterly random at first glance, with one set of clues comprising Numbers, Thunderball, Cuthbert, and Sailor(n)

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The quarter-final matches continue as two more teams of four students compete to make it to the next stage of the competition as they try to win two matches and progress to the semi-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions(n)

programme.title: Miriam's Big Fat Adventure
programme.desc: Part one of two. Actress Miriam Margolyes travels around the nation to discover why so many people are overweight, despite so much information about healthy lifestyles. She wants to understand what impact being obese has on people and how the rest of society views them and begins by meeting people fighting back against their size - both physically and psychologically(n)

programme.title: Inside No 9
programme.desc: Two police officers always have time to get to know each other while working on the night shift together. However, PC Thompson's previous partner died in a brutal attack less than a month previously, the forensic evidence has gone missing and PCSO Varney is beginning to ask a lot of awkward questions. The final instalment of the fifth series of the anthology of darkly comic tales, written by and starring The League of Gentlemen and Psychoville's Steve Pemberton and Reece Shearsmith, with Rebecca Callard and Malik Ibheis(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's important national and international news stories, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: The documentary focuses on the work of staff at Alder Hey Children's Hospital. Eight-month-old Elsie was not expected to survive being born due to serious kidney abnormalities. But she has spent all but two weeks of her life in hospital and has 11 operations to repair the damage caused by a genetic problem. Now she faces surgery yet again. Holly has cerebral palsy and, as she is about to turn 18, is preparing to transition to adult services in Liverpool - a huge leap for her parents who have grown to depend on the hospital's care(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef embarks on another round of extended culinary breaks, visiting markets, restaurants, wineries, cafes and bars, beginning with Portuguese capital Lisbon. The culinary effects of Portugal's historic explorations are still seen in its dishes with hints of cinnamon and freshly chopped coriander from the east and tomatoes and chillies from the Americas. Rick is inspired to cook salt cod fritters and an almond tart, before heading for the St Anthony's Day Parade and Sardine Festival(n)

programme.title: South Pacific
programme.desc: An exploration of the effects of the eruption of volcanos in the ocean. From these violent beginnings emerge islands and coral reefs that are home to a vast array of wildlife, including giant manta rays and vampire bugs that thrive in the tropical snow, after a process that takes millions of years. Benedict Cumberbatch narrates(n)

programme.title: Hidden
programme.desc: Karl Lewis admits while questioned by Cadi that the accusations he brought against Ellis were false. Cadi turns her attention to Mia, whose false testimony led to Karl's arrest. Crime drama, starring Sian Reese-Williams. In English and Welsh(n)

programme.title: Clive James: Postcard from Miami
programme.desc: Clive sets out to discover the truth about an American city that was seen as the vice capital of America, teeming with money, illegal aliens and cocaine. He investigates what is beneath the surface, meeting famous resdients in Miami Vice star Don Johnson and singer Gloria Estefan(n)

programme.title: Clive James: Postcard from Chicago
programme.desc: The writer visits the Windy City and finds there is a lot more to Chicago than memories of Al Capone and the St Valentine's Day Massacre. The people are surprisingly forthright about the problems of living in a city where millions of dollars change hands at the board of trade, but little is done to solve the problems of gang violence in the housing projects where shootings are a daily event(n)

programme.title: The Selecter and EOB at the 6 Music Festival
programme.desc: Performances from the Roundhouse in Camden by the veteran ska band led by Pauline Black, and Radiohead guitarist Ed O'Brien's new solo project EOB(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell and Sybil Ruscoe host the edition first shown on April 20, featuring performances by Transvision Vamp, Inner City, Holly Johnson, Metallica, Midnight Oil, the Cure, Simple Minds, the Bangles and the Beatmasters with Merlin(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Gary Davies hosts the edition first shown on April 27, featuring performances by London Boys, Natalie Cole, De la Soul, Fine Young Cannibals, Morrissey, the Beatmasters with Merlin, Yazz, the Bangles and Poison(n)

programme.title: South Pacific
programme.desc: An exploration of the effects of the eruption of volcanos in the ocean. From these violent beginnings emerge islands and coral reefs that are home to a vast array of wildlife, including giant manta rays and vampire bugs that thrive in the tropical snow, after a process that takes millions of years. Benedict Cumberbatch narrates(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef embarks on another round of extended culinary breaks, visiting markets, restaurants, wineries, cafes and bars, beginning with Portuguese capital Lisbon. The culinary effects of Portugal's historic explorations are still seen in its dishes with hints of cinnamon and freshly chopped coriander from the east and tomatoes and chillies from the Americas. Rick is inspired to cook salt cod fritters and an almond tart, before heading for the St Anthony's Day Parade and Sardine Festival(n)

programme.title: West Coast Otters
programme.desc: Portrait of two otters living on the idyllic west coast of Scotland. An intimate relationship between the inseparable mother and daughter is observed. The cub is seen learning to fend for herself and, as she faces the dangers of her first Scottish winter, mum must work hard to ensure they both survive. Narrated by Pam Ferris(n)

programme.title: Live Women's International Football
programme.desc: Japan Women v England Women. Eilidh Barbour presents coverage of the second half of the latest match in the round-robin competition, held at Red Bull Arena in Harrison, New Jersey, as England continue their defence of the trophy. Phil Neville's side recorded an impressive 3-0 victory over Japan last year to secure the title, but they have not been at their best since the 2019 World Cup concluded and will be looking to get back on track before hosting Euro 2021(n)

programme.title: Bloody Queens: Elizabeth and Mary
programme.desc: A dramatisation based on the exchange of letters between Mary Queen of Scots and her cousin Elizabeth I, detailing the hatred and obsession in their bitter rivalry. Expert historians examine and interpret the royals' motives for the animosity that lasted more than two decades, and which threatened to tear apart the reigning monarch and her kingdom(n)

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of how 24-year-old Tracy Edwards became the skipper of the first all-female crew to enter the Whitbread Round the World Race in 1989. The film reveals how her male competitors thought an all-women crew would never make it, while the chauvinistic yachting press took bets on her failing, and potential sponsors rejected her, fearing the crew would die at sea and generate bad publicity(n)

programme.title: What Do Artists Do All Day?
programme.desc: The daily life of children's book illustrator Shirley Hughes, who chats about her love of drawing and the challenges of coming up with new ideas(n)

programme.title: 6 Music Festival Celebrates International Women's Day
programme.desc: Lauren Laverne presents from the Roundhouse in Camden, on International Women's Day, featuring live performances by Roisin Murphy, Kate Tempest, Kim Gordon and Nadine Shah(n)

programme.title: Unsung Heroines: Danielle de Niese on the Lost World of Female Composers
programme.desc: The soprano explores the lives and works of five female composers, from the middle ages to the late 20th century, who were famous in their lifetimes but whose work was then forgotten. Traditionally, western classical music is seen as a procession of male geniuses, but the truth is that women have always composed. Hildegard of Bingen, Francesca Caccini, Clara Schumann, Florence Price and Elizabeth Maconchy all battled to fulfil their ambitions and overcome the obstacles that society placed in their way, but then disappeared into obscurity and only some have found recognition again. Contributors include pianist Lucy Parham(n)

programme.title: Darcey Bussell: Looking for Margot
programme.desc: Margot Fonteyn inspired generations of ballerinas, while her late-flowering partnership with Rudolf Nureyev created the most dazzling ballet partnership in history. However, her life was marked by tragedy and disappointment. Darcey finds that Margot couldn't find love and never had children, got drawn into a failed foreign revolution, danced on for too long, and died in poverty. The former ballerina goes behind the scenes at the Royal Opera House and the Royal Ballet, and travels from London to New York and Panama, discovering that Margot did eventually find a kind of happiness - though one that came at a high price(n)

programme.title: Kiri Te Kanawa at the BBC
programme.desc: Highlights of the soprano's career, over the past 40 years. The programme showcases memorable performances, including the Last Night of the Proms, the Royal Opera's production of Puccini's Manon, and singing rugby union anthem World in Union on Top of the Pops. Plus, chat show appearances and documentary footage in which the singer returns to her roots in New Zealand and discusses performing at a royal wedding in 1981(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Francesco's Mediterranean Voyage
programme.desc: Francesco da Mosto follows the modern-day pilgrimage route to the island of Tinos and its reputedly miraculous statue of the Virgin Mary as he sails through the Cyclades archipelago. On Delos, he investigates the temples of a more ancient religion, while Naxos is the site for an octopus fishing expedition(n)

programme.title: Dynasties
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a documentary following five of the world's most endangered species as they struggle to preserve their social groups, beginning with a chimpanzee troop in Senegal. As the dry season sets in, the Alpha of the pack, who has ruled unchallenged for the past three years, faces a series of challenges and finds little support from the other apes(n)

programme.title: Age of the Image
programme.desc: Art historian James Fox explores how mass communication and new technology helped 20th-century image-makers transform society, as films, photographs, TV, art and advertising all became weapons in the ideological battles of the age. He reveals how actress Marlene Dietrich and director Leni Riefenstahl each used cinema to pursue very different visions of power and freedom, and how Jewish comic book artists in New York created superheroes as their act of resistance to the Nazi threat(n)

programme.title: Suffragettes with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: To mark 100 years since women over the age of 30, who owned property, were first allowed to vote in the UK, Lucy Worsley examines the battle that women had to wage for that right. She tells the story a group of less well-known young working-class suffragettes who decided to go against every rule and expectation that Edwardian society had about them, and ended up being tracked by a special police unit(n)

programme.title: Art of France
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon examines the development of Impressionism, when France was changing at a rapid pace and young artists re-invented the rules of painting - finding their muses in the bars, brothels and cabarets of Paris and turning the world of art on its head. Monet, Degas and their contemporaries launched a heated debate about the role of painting in the modern world that would pave the way for just about every modern art movement of note(n)

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Animated antics with the adventurous aardvark and his friends(n)

Just four performers remain in the contest, and with the help of the dance coaches Cheryl, Matthew Morrison, Oti Mabuse and Todrick Hall, they will put on one last show to impress the studio audience and win £50,000 and a chance to showcase their skills on Strictly Come Dancing. Who will become The Greatest Dancer? Plus, another world class guest artist performs a unique collaboration(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Contestants include Prue Leith, Brian Turner, Grace Dent, William Sitwell, Hala El-Shafie, Ed Baines, Michael Caines and Anna Haugh(n)

programme.title: Catchpoint
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts a celebrity special edition of the family-friendly game show combining physical fun and an exciting question mechanic, as cricketer Graeme Swann and actress Rachel Parris take on Olympic hockey player Sam Quek and comedian Mark Watson(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Ruby is back at work but she is losing patience with her mother, Lavender, and has to choose between her career and parenthood. Elsewhere, Megan offers Archie a job with her in Sudan, but she is not sure if she wants to leave, and Jade finally loses her patience with Marty when he is late for another shift(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents action from the latest Premier League fixtures, including Liverpool v Bournemouth at Anfield and Arsenal v West Ham United. The Reds were expected to move a step closer to the title with victory over the Cherries, while Arsenal were aiming to complete a double over their London rivals after beating them 3-1 at London Stadium in December. Plus, Crystal Palace v Watford, Sheffield United v Norwich City, Southampton v Newcastle United, Wolverhampton Wanderers v Brighton & Hove Albion and Burnley v Tottenham Hotspur(n)

programme.title: Foxcatcher (2014)
programme.desc: A reclusive, eccentric millionaire persuades an Olympic gold-medal-winning wrestler to join his personal team. Initially flattered by the attention lavished upon him by his wealthy new friend, the grappler flourishes - but tension soon grows between them when his brother is employed as a coach, a situation that leads to tragedy. Fact-based drama, starring Steve Carell, Channing Tatum and Mark Ruffalo(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Ben Thompson and Nina Warhurst present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents action from the latest Premier League fixtures, including Liverpool v Bournemouth at Anfield and Arsenal v West Ham United. The Reds were expected to move a step closer to the title with victory over the Cherries, while Arsenal were aiming to complete a double over their London rivals after beating them 3-1 at London Stadium in December. Plus, Crystal Palace v Watford, Sheffield United v Norwich City, Southampton v Newcastle United, Wolverhampton Wanderers v Brighton & Hove Albion and Burnley v Tottenham Hotspur(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Politics Wales
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate and reports from around Wales, presented by James Williams(n)

programme.title: The Big Questions
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell presents moral and religious discussions on topical issues, with contributions by members of the audience at Oasis Academy MediaCityUK in Salford(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp takes the bargain hunting teams to Leominster, Herefordshire, where two teams compete to buy three items that will hopefully make the greater profit at auction. Providing a helping hand to the sides are experts Paul Laidlaw and Mark Ashley. Natasha also meets Royal Worcester aficionado Henry Sandon(n)

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway is on Gower, but instead of taking the route along Worms Head sets off along a less well-known stretch of coast from Fall Bay to Oxwich. The first half of the walk is along the headland so, to get a closer look at the cliffs, Derek joins a group abseiling into a blow hole and squeezing through a tunnel. Re-joining the pathway, Derek then learns about the area's history of smuggling and deliberate shipwrecking. After walking round the Oxwich headland, he arrives at pretty St Illytd's church. His walk ends on Oxwich beach, where he forages for seaweed with top chef Hywel Griffith(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins celebrates International Women's Day with hymns by female writers, and hears about author Michelle Sloan new children's book featuring women from the Bible(n)

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents a look ahead to France v Scotland, assessing the team selections and potential tactics that will be employed by each side(n)

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: Scotland v France (Kick-off 3.00pm). John Inverdale presents coverage of both teams' penultimate match of the championship, held at Murrayfield. Scotland recorded their first win of the campaign last time out against Italy and have won their last three home games against the French. However, Les Bleus have been in excellent form so far in this season's competition and are looking to move a step closer to completing the Grand Slam. With analysis from Andy Nicol, John Barclay, Thomas Castaignede and Paul O'Connell, and commentary by Andrew Cotter, Chris Paterson and Brian Moore(n)

programme.title: Shane Williams: Rugby, Concussion and Me
programme.desc: Shane Williams heads on a journey to understand how the world of rugby, from grass roots to the highest levels, is working to reduce concussions in the modern-day game and helping to make the sport safer for future generations. He talks to players past and present, the custodians of the game at World Rugby and leading global medical professionals studying the long-term effects of contact sport on the brain(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team marks International Women's Day on the Holnicote Estate in Somerset, where Anita Rani meets Holly Purdley from the Women in Farming group, which aims to reduce the isolation often felt by women who live and work on farms. Matt Baker finds out about as innovative river restoration project that is also home to a new colony of beavers. Plus, Hannah Jackson - aka the Red Shepherdess - looks at whether times have really changed for women in farming(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents from the grounds of Salisbury Cathedral in Wiltshire, where the experts value decorative objects and rarities belonging to the public. An eclectic mix includes one of Napoleon's razors, letters from Enid Blyton and an item of Queen Victoria's underwear, as well as a hand-painted Christmas card from the Second World War and original drawings of Judge Dredd(n)

programme.title: Last Tango in Halifax
programme.desc: There is chaos at the bungalow as work begins to strip out the old kitchen, while Alan's new supermarket job brings an unexpected challenge in the form of Harrison. An overburdened Celia is called upon to travel by bus, while the giraffe continues to torment Gillian, who is pushed to her wits' end by the return of PC Cheryl. Elsewhere, a hungover Judith has an epiphany, Caroline gains an unwanted lodger, and the truth about Ted's last-minute trip comes to light.(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents action from today's Premier League fixtures - Manchester United v Manchester City at Old Trafford and Chelsea v Everton at Stamford Bridge. United were looking to complete a double over their local rivals after winning 2-1 at Etihad Stadium in December, while Carlo Ancelotti was facing his former club for the first time since taking over Everton. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's top-flight goals(n)

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Newlyweds Dermot and Maria argue over whether they should buy their own home. Meanwhile, Grandad hopes to witness his own funeral, but Mrs Brown's plans go awry when she mistakes two Mormons - who have been stealing Father Quinn's parishioners - for the men who are supposed to be delivering the coffin(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Outspoken funnyman Frankie Boyle hosts an evening of stand-up at the Apollo in Hammersmith, London, treating the audience to his own brand of humour before handing over to the acerbic Simon Evans and actress/stand-up Aisling Bea(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Rav Wilding returns with more appeals for information about crimes, with roving reporter Michelle Ackerley out and about once more, today joining firefighters at a specialist training centre in Darlington(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The team visits a couple who bought a prefabricated bungalow in Chesterton, Staffordshire, as well as first-time developers in Sutton in Surrey and Bilston in the West Midlands. Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell present(n)

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood spends 24 hours sampling some of the US's most dangerous and demanding jobs, beginning by working as a correctional officer in a Texas jail(n)

programme.title: Saved on Camera
programme.desc: The Coastguard is called to rescue a paraglider who crashed into a cliff, while a special robot helps save a spaniel that had been stuck in a pipe for three days(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning and Christina Trevanion present from the Shropshire town of Oswestry, where two teams shop for antiques with the help of experts Kate Bliss and Ochuko Ojiri. Anita also meets Per Lindstrand, a pioneer who made history with his balloon flights(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Daniel and Zara have fun trying to keep a secret, while a determined Bear returns from his break. Al tries to help the owner of his favourite pub, whose life is falling apart(n)

programme.title: A Service of Celebration for Commonwealth Day
programme.desc: In the presence of the Queen and other senior members of the royal family, Petroc Trelawny presents live coverage from Westminster Abbey of a service featuring readings, music and dance. Including performances by Alexandra Burke, Craig David and the Melodian steel band, as well as a reflection by world heavyweight boxing champion Anthony Joshua. Sonali Shah interviews honoured guests and participants(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore rescues two items from a recycling centre in Witley, Surrey with creative duo Josh and Oli coming up with an idea that could make a fantastic profit(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Friends and former scout leaders Anne and Jenny take part in the culinary contest, teaming up with chefs Romy Gill and Akis Petretzikis. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Matt Baker present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: X-Ray
programme.desc: Rachel Treadaway-Williams takes up the case of a motorist facing a £10,000 bill because of a £10 oil filter, while Omar Hamdi looks at the fashion for alternative milks. Lucy Owen meets investigators fighting fly-tipping at a Welsh beauty spot, and the team hears complaints from students about the way a lettings agent shows people around their homes(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Chantelle comforts Gray when his office returns his belongings although he cannot face telling Whitney what has happened. Jean accuses Suki of lying about her illness, leaving her family disgusted that she could suggest such a thing. Linda is hurt that Mick doesn't trust her when she finds him searching for stashed alcohol. Jay makes a big decision about his future(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The Department for Work and Pensions is meant to help disabled people get back into work. In this programme, reporter Richard Butchins discovers that the DWP has lost more employment tribunals for disability discrimination than any other employer in Britain. He investigates why the DWP has paid its own employees nearly a million pounds of public money in both tribunal pay-outs and out-of-court settlements(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace preside as six more hopefuls compete in the third week of heats. They are challenged to serve up their signature dish, with the judges' favourite two winning a coveted MasterChef apron and a place in the next round. The remaining four must come up with a dish using three specific ingredients, after which two cooks are sent home - leaving the remaining four to cook two courses for former contestants who have gone on to work in the food industry(n)

programme.title: Young, Welsh and Pretty Minted
programme.desc: The programme meets 20-year-old Callum Griffiths, who is one of Europe's up-and-coming suppliers of dog biscuits, and designer Ibby Tarafdar, who spends half of his time jet-setting to meet his famous American clients, and the other half working as a barista. Instagram star Erin Budina is branching out with a YouTube channel that challenges her to do everyday jobs - but will working in a recycling depot prove a step too far for her?(n)

programme.title: This Country
programme.desc: Kurtan receives some sad news about a relative and endeavours to make some new memories. Meanwhile, Kerry discovers a passion for reading and joins the vicar's book club, which goes well until another village resident joins. Spoof documentary, starring Daisy May and Charlie Cooper(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Eddie Izzard hosts an evening of stand-up at the Apollo in Hammersmith, London, treating the audience to his own brand of stream-of-consciousness humour before introducing routines by The Last Leg star Josh Widdicombe and South African comic Trevor Noah(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Rachel Burden present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Glynn Purnell is joined by chefs Tom Kitchin and Georgina Hayden, and Sir Chris Hoy faces his food heaven or hell. Plus, expert Olly Smith picks wines to go with the studio dishes(n)

programme.title: Gareth Thomas' Tour de Trophy for Sport Relief
programme.desc: Rugby star Gareth Thomas embarks on a seven day, 500-mile bike ride from Cardiff to Aberdeen, towing the 40kg BBC Sports Personality of the Year trophy for Sport Relief. Gareth allows cameras record every minute of his struggle, in the hope that this film would help change attitudes to people either living in isolation or burdened by a social stigma, issues close to Gareth's heart(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's football, including Arsenal v West Ham United. Plus, news from the lunchtime game between Liverpool and Bournemouth(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts a revival of the culinary duel in which two chefs create a meal in 20 minutes using ingredients brought in by the contestants helping them. In the first edition, Rylan unveils the new kitchen and introduces chefs Anna Haugh and Mike Reid, who are joined by Falkirk couple Gemma and Brian(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich visit Mansfield and Redditch to help single mum Holly and Annette, who moved into her home with her three sons two years ago(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore enlists carpenter Bruce Faulseit to repurpose an old set of coat hooks, while woodworker Norman Wilkinson tackles two chairs in need of a makeover, an creative duo Josh and Oli have the unusual task of reimagining a collection of fishing rods(n)

programme.title: Sportscene Results
programme.desc: David Currie presents the classified results and a round-up of the afternoon's main football news. Plus, a look back at the week's talking points(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team takes on a much-loved landscape hanging together by a few threads, a treasured clockwork car and a porcelain dog damaged by a real canine(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys tests four more celebrities in the famous black chair. programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: Footage of wildlife inhabiting underwater kelp forests, including thousands of giant cuttlefish spawning along a restricted area of rocky reef off the south coast of Australia. Males outnumber females 11 to one, which leads to fierce competition. Larger males use brute force to drive off competition, while their smaller rivals use deception by mimicking the appearance of females. The programme also features tiger sharks hunting for green turtles in fields of seagrass and spider crabs trying to avoid predators while they shed their shells(n)

programme.title: Arctic Academy
programme.desc: Documentary following 10 Scots aged between 13 and 15 as they embark on the school trip of a lifetime. It begins with 20 youngsters from Bathgate Academy in West Lothian as they are put through their paces by explorer and Polar Academy founder Craig Mathieson, before the final 10 are selected. The young people have been whittled down from an initial group of 200 who applied to take part in a gruelling expedition through the freezing Arctic wilderness of Greenland, with only 10 being selected to take part(n)
