programme.desc: Rugby star Gareth Thomas embarks on a seven day, 500-mile bike ride from Cardiff to Aberdeen, towing the 40kg BBC Sports Personality of the Year trophy for Sport Relief. Gareth allows cameras record every minute of his struggle, in the hope that this film would help change attitudes to people either living in isolation or burdened by a social stigma, issues close to Gareth's heart(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's football, including Arsenal v West Ham United. Plus, news from the lunchtime game between Liverpool and Bournemouth(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts a revival of the culinary duel in which two chefs create a meal in 20 minutes using ingredients brought in by the contestants helping them. In the first edition, Rylan unveils the new kitchen and introduces chefs Anna Haugh and Mike Reid, who are joined by Falkirk couple Gemma and Brian(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich visit Mansfield and Redditch to help single mum Holly and Annette, who moved into her home with her three sons two years ago(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore enlists carpenter Bruce Faulseit to repurpose an old set of coat hooks, while woodworker Norman Wilkinson tackles two chairs in need of a makeover, an creative duo Josh and Oli have the unusual task of reimagining a collection of fishing rods(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland(n)

programme.title: Final Score from Northern Ireland
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's action along with the classified results(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team takes on a much-loved landscape hanging together by a few threads, a treasured clockwork car and a porcelain dog damaged by a real canine(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys tests four more celebrities in the famous black chair. Taking part tonight are TV presenter Radzi Chinyanganya, actress Ruth Madeley, politician Ann Widdecombe and TV personality Tom Read Wilson, who answer questions on Olympic sprinters, Will and Grace, The Escape of King Charles II and My Fair Lady(n)

programme.title: The Greatest Dancer
programme.desc: Alesha Dixon and Jordan Banjo host the final of the dancing contest. Just four performers remain in the contest, and with the help of the dance coaches Cheryl, Matthew Morrison, Oti Mabuse and Todrick Hall, they will put on one last show to impress the studio audience and win £50,000 and a chance to showcase their skills on Strictly Come Dancing. Who will become The Greatest Dancer? Plus, another world class guest artist performs a unique collaboration(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Contestants include Prue Leith, Brian Turner, Grace Dent, William Sitwell, Hala El-Shafie, Ed Baines, Michael Caines and Anna Haugh(n)

programme.title: Catchpoint
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts a celebrity special edition of the family-friendly game show combining physical fun and an exciting question mechanic, as cricketer Graeme Swann and actress Rachel Parris take on Olympic hockey player Sam Quek and comedian Mark Watson(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Ruby is back at work but she is losing patience with her mother, Lavender, and has to choose between her career and parenthood. Elsewhere, Megan offers Archie a job with her in Sudan, but she is not sure if she wants to leave, and Jade finally loses her patience with Marty when he is late for another shift(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents action from the latest Premier League fixtures, including Liverpool v Bournemouth at Anfield and Arsenal v West Ham United. The Reds were expected to move a step closer to the title with victory over the Cherries, while Arsenal were aiming to complete a double over their London rivals after beating them 3-1 at London Stadium in December. Plus, Crystal Palace v Watford, Sheffield United v Norwich City, Southampton v Newcastle United, Wolverhampton Wanderers v Brighton & Hove Albion and Burnley v Tottenham Hotspur(n)

programme.title: Foxcatcher (2014)
programme.desc: A reclusive, eccentric millionaire persuades an Olympic gold-medal-winning wrestler to join his personal team. Initially flattered by the attention lavished upon him by his wealthy new friend, the grappler flourishes - but tension soon grows between them when his brother is employed as a coach, a situation that leads to tragedy. Fact-based drama, starring Steve Carell, Channing Tatum and Mark Ruffalo(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Ben Thompson and Nina Warhurst present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents action from the latest Premier League fixtures, including Liverpool v Bournemouth at Anfield and Arsenal v West Ham United. The Reds were expected to move a step closer to the title with victory over the Cherries, while Arsenal were aiming to complete a double over their London rivals after beating them 3-1 at London Stadium in December. Plus, Crystal Palace v Watford, Sheffield United v Norwich City, Southampton v Newcastle United, Wolverhampton Wanderers v Brighton & Hove Albion and Burnley v Tottenham Hotspur(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Sunday Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers looks at the political developments of the week and questions policy-makers on the key issues(n)

programme.title: The Big Questions
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell presents moral and religious discussions on topical issues, with contributions by members of the audience at Oasis Academy MediaCityUK in Salford(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Tim Weeks and Colin Young help two teams find items at an antique centre in Elsecar, South Yorkshire, while presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp tries her hand at sword dancing(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts as mother and daughter Azra and Hannah do battle in the kitchen, with the help of chefs Ellis Barrie and Akis Petretzikis(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins celebrates International Women's Day with hymns by female writers, and hears about author Michelle Sloan new children's book featuring women from the Bible(n)

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents a look ahead to France v Scotland, assessing the team selections and potential tactics that will be employed by each side(n)

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: Scotland v France (Kick-off 3.00pm). John Inverdale presents coverage of both teams' penultimate match of the championship, held at Murrayfield. Scotland recorded their first win of the campaign last time out against Italy and have won their last three home games against the French. However, Les Bleus have been in excellent form so far in this season's competition and are looking to move a step closer to completing the Grand Slam. With analysis from Andy Nicol, John Barclay, Thomas Castaignede and Paul O'Connell, and commentary by Andrew Cotter, Chris Paterson and Brian Moore(n)

programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: Footage of wildlife inhabiting underwater kelp forests, including thousands of giant cuttlefish spawning along a restricted area of rocky reef off the south coast of Australia. Males outnumber females 11 to one, which leads to fierce competition. Larger males use brute force to drive off competition, while their smaller rivals use deception by mimicking the appearance of females. The programme also features tiger sharks hunting for green turtles in fields of seagrass and spider crabs trying to avoid predators while they shed their shells(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team marks International Women's Day on the Holnicote Estate in Somerset, where Anita Rani meets Holly Purdley from the Women in Farming group, which aims to reduce the isolation often felt by women who live and work on farms. Matt Baker finds out about as innovative river restoration project that is also home to a new colony of beavers. Plus, Hannah Jackson - aka the Red Shepherdess - looks at whether times have really changed for women in farming(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents from the grounds of Salisbury Cathedral in Wiltshire, where the experts value decorative objects and rarities belonging to the public. An eclectic mix includes one of Napoleon's razors, letters from Enid Blyton and an item of Queen Victoria's underwear, as well as a hand-painted Christmas card from the Second World War and original drawings of Judge Dredd(n)

programme.title: Last Tango in Halifax
programme.desc: There is chaos at the bungalow as work begins to strip out the old kitchen, while Alan's new supermarket job brings an unexpected challenge in the form of Harrison. An overburdened Celia is called upon to travel by bus, while the giraffe continues to torment Gillian, who is pushed to her wits' end by the return of PC Cheryl. Elsewhere, a hungover Judith has an epiphany, Caroline gains an unwanted lodger, and the truth about Ted's last-minute trip comes to light.(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents action from today's Premier League fixtures - Manchester United v Manchester City at Old Trafford and Chelsea v Everton at Stamford Bridge. United were looking to complete a double over their local rivals after winning 2-1 at Etihad Stadium in December, while Carlo Ancelotti was facing his former club for the first time since taking over Everton. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's top-flight goals(n)

programme.title: Shane Williams: Rugby, Concussion and Me
programme.desc: Shane Williams heads on a journey to understand how the world of rugby, from grass roots to the highest levels, is working to reduce concussions in the modern-day game and helping to make the sport safer for future generations. He talks to players past and present, the custodians of the game at World Rugby and leading global medical professionals studying the long-term effects of contact sport on the brain(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Rav Wilding returns with more appeals for information about crimes, with roving reporter Michelle Ackerley out and about once more, today joining firefighters at a specialist training centre in Darlington(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The team visits a couple who bought a prefabricated bungalow in Chesterton, Staffordshire, as well as first-time developers in Sutton in Surrey and Bilston in the West Midlands. Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell present(n)

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood spends 24 hours sampling some of the US's most dangerous and demanding jobs, beginning by working as a correctional officer in a Texas jail(n)

programme.title: Saved on Camera
programme.desc: The Coastguard is called to rescue a paraglider who crashed into a cliff, while a special robot helps save a spaniel that had been stuck in a pipe for three days(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning and Christina Trevanion present from the Shropshire town of Oswestry, where two teams shop for antiques with the help of experts Kate Bliss and Ochuko Ojiri. Anita also meets Per Lindstrand, a pioneer who made history with his balloon flights(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Daniel and Zara have fun trying to keep a secret, while a determined Bear returns from his break. Al tries to help the owner of his favourite pub, whose life is falling apart(n)

programme.title: A Service of Celebration for Commonwealth Day
programme.desc: In the presence of the Queen and other senior members of the royal family, Petroc Trelawny presents live coverage from Westminster Abbey of a service featuring readings, music and dance. Including performances by Alexandra Burke, Craig David and the Melodian steel band, as well as a reflection by world heavyweight boxing champion Anthony Joshua. Sonali Shah interviews honoured guests and participants(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore rescues two items from a recycling centre in Witley, Surrey with creative duo Josh and Oli coming up with an idea that could make a fantastic profit(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Friends and former scout leaders Anne and Jenny take part in the culinary contest, teaming up with chefs Romy Gill and Akis Petretzikis. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Matt Baker present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: The Chronicles of Erne
programme.desc: The programme documents autumn on the lough, with Frankie and Eddie Roofe taking part in the annual Erne Pike Classic fishing competition and Joe Kelly juggling running an artisan bakery with spending as much time as possible on his parents' island(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Chantelle comforts Gray when his office returns his belongings although he cannot face telling Whitney what has happened. Jean accuses Suki of lying about her illness, leaving her family disgusted that she could suggest such a thing. Linda is hurt that Mick doesn't trust her when she finds him searching for stashed alcohol. Jay makes a big decision about his future(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The Department for Work and Pensions is meant to help disabled people get back into work. In this programme, reporter Richard Butchins discovers that the DWP has lost more employment tribunals for disability discrimination than any other employer in Britain. He investigates why the DWP has paid its own employees nearly a million pounds of public money in both tribunal pay-outs and out-of-court settlements(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace preside as six more hopefuls compete in the third week of heats. They are challenged to serve up their signature dish, with the judges' favourite two winning a coveted MasterChef apron and a place in the next round. The remaining four must come up with a dish using three specific ingredients, after which two cooks are sent home - leaving the remaining four to cook two courses for former contestants who have gone on to work in the food industry(n)

programme.title: There's No Place Like Tyrone
programme.desc: Lynda challenges her partner Bobby to spice up date night, while Ruth and her dad John go shopping for a new beauty salon, albeit in very different places(n)

programme.title: This Country
programme.desc: Kurtan receives some sad news about a relative and endeavours to make some new memories. Meanwhile, Kerry discovers a passion for reading and joins the vicar's book club, which goes well until another village resident joins. Spoof documentary, starring Daisy May and Charlie Cooper(n)

programme.title: Young, Welsh and Pretty Minted
programme.desc: The programme meets 20-year-old Callum Griffiths, who is one of Europe's up-and-coming suppliers of dog biscuits, and designer Ibby Tarafdar, who spends half of his time jet-setting to meet his famous American clients, and the other half working as a barista. Instagram star Erin Budina is branching out with a YouTube channel that challenges her to do everyday jobs - but will working in a recycling depot prove a step too far for her?(n)

programme.desc: House of Commons proceedings in Westminster Hall from Tuesday 3 March.(n)

programme.title: Our World
programme.desc: Colombia has long been the world's largest producer of cocaine. A historic peace deal in 2016 was meant to reduce the amount of cocaine being produced by offering farmers alternatives to growing coca. But last year the UN estimated that its output was the largest since records began. Our World travelled to the Cauca Valley to find that farmers are now caught between new criminal gangs with devastating consequences(n)

After that one pair will be eliminated, with the other going on to the final round - performing their own take on Torvill and Dean's signature piece, Ravel's Bolero. programme.title: Take Me Out
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness invites more would-be Romeos to enters the 'love lift' and claim the hearts of his 30 single women. This time, the men seeking to win a date are Louis, a debt collector from Great Yarmouth, Tenby-based tugboat skipper Patrick, Thomas, a chorister from Arundel, and Oxford stunt-man Jon. Each will be seeking a companion to whisk off to the Isle of Fernandos, but first they must get the female participants to keep their lights on as a sign of approval(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, featuring a child getting trapped in a deckchair, a cat falling through a ceiling, and a parachutist losing his false teeth(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers. An indoor golfer wrecks his home, a lion pees on a cameraman and a man flies a drone into his own face. Plus, a new inflatable to solve Britain's border control issues post-Brexit(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, featuring everything from giddy grandmas, cute kids and crazy animals to below-the-belt mishaps, practical jokers and painful-looking accidents(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, featuring everything from giddy grandmas, cute kids and crazy animals to below-the-belt mishaps, practical jokers and painful-looking accidents(n)

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Walden and Alan work together to plan a bachelor party to remember for Marty after he pops the question to Evelyn. However, things take a turn for the worse when he suddenly calls off the wedding. Comedy, starring Ashton Kutcher and Jon Cryer(n)

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Walden worries that his new girlfriend is cheating on him. Meanwhile, Alan gets even more intertwined in Lyndsey and Larry's relationship when he starts dating Larry's sister Gretchen. Sitcom, starring Ashton Kutcher and Jon Cryer(n)

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter and Lois send Chris to space camp to boost his self-esteem. When his stint ends and the Griffins arrive to pick him up, they are accidentally launched into space and it is down to the awkward youth to land the clan safely back on Earth(n)

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: While in search of an identity, Jeff starts hanging out with Stan's favourite celebrity chef. Meanwhile, Klaus gets an exciting new outfit(n)

programme.title: The Stand Up Sketch Show
programme.desc: Routines by comedians including Nigel Ng, Helen Bauer, Lily Phillips and Seann Walsh are reconstructed as sketches(n)

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian writes a play that becomes a hit in Quahog, but loses his confidence when he finds that Stewie is also a budding playwright and is better at it. When the toddler sees how upset his friend is, he decides to make amends. Animation, featuring the guest voice of playwright Alan Bennett as himself(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The detective investigates after a botanist accidentally unearths the body of a recently buried professor who was fixated upon solving a seemingly impossible riddle by Lewis Carroll. The victim had a long-standing rivalry with his brother - giving Lewis and Hathaway an obvious suspect - but the case is hindered by the mind games of two students who seem intent on causing trouble in their quest to gain admittance to a mysterious club. Guest starring Celia Imrie and James Fleet, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster teams up with a dashing man to investigate a series of poison-pen letters in the sleepy village of Lymstock. Two of the missives have led to the deaths of Mona Symmington, the wife of Lymstock solicitor Richard, and Agnes Woddell, the parlour maid at the Symmingtons' house. However, Miss Marple suspects there is more to these deaths than meets the eye. Murder mystery with an all-star cast, featuring Geraldine McEwan, James D'Arcy, Emilia Fox, Imogen Stubbs, Ken Russell, Harry Enfield and Kelly Brook(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: The Barlows and Platts convene for Adam and Sarah's wedding. Tyrone and Fiz unite against Jade. Gary bumps into an old acquaintance(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Jones investigate the death of a local aristocrat who seemingly died of a coronary - shortly after declaring he had an important announcement to make. After the peer's will disappears and his lawyer is killed in an arson attack, it is clear sinister forces are at work. However, the prime suspects all appear to have cast-iron alibis. Guest starring Joss Ackland and Sian Phillips, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: While investigating the shooting of a council clerk, Barnaby and Jones discover he was an expert blackmailer. A phone number on the dead man's blotter sends the duo to the seaside in search of his latest targets - whose boat trip could conceal a motive for the killing. Meanwhile, Joyce is convinced a local farmer knows more than he is telling. Julia McKenzie and Grant Masters guest star, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Jones investigate the deaths of a female pensioner and a vicar who were both killed during Broughton village's annual contest between men and women. The pair soon discover the preacher had a secret that could provide a motive - but when another body is found, they realise the answer to all the deaths lies in the history books. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Winter and Barnaby investigate when Lower Pampling's star cricketer Leo Henderson is found dead after a match in the grounds, pummelled to death by cricket balls. Barnaby is surprised to see DS Jones undercover as one of Leo's team-mates, and his former colleague reveals he is working on suspected cases of match-fixing in the area, but things take an even more sinister turn when captain Fitz Theara is stabbed through the heart with a cricket stump(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: DCI Barnaby and DS Winter discover a dead body surrounded by rabbits that have escaped from a local pet show, after someone deliberately freed them from their cages. At the crime scene, DCI Barnaby and DS Winter discover that the victim died from a stab wound to the neck, and theorise that the murderer might be a competitor sabotaging the Belville Small Pet Show, and question the Best in Show defending champion Timothy Benson. The pair must work out if this is a sign of rivalry taken too far, or is the killer driven by something darker?(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: As Foyle prepares to be replaced at the police station and begin retirement, his attention is grabbed by a news story about a man sentenced to hang for high treason, and he decides to take on the case. But with the prisoner's execution date approaching, the detective has to work fast to find evidence that will clear him. Meanwhile, Samantha and Adam fight to save Hill House from the town planners determined to demolish it - but disaster soon strikes. Starring Michael Kitchen, Honeysuckle Weeks and Max Brown(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Janet moves a step closer to tracking down Veronica's killer while investigating a recent murder that bears similarities to the cold case. Rachel finds out about an affair Nick once had with a juror in the middle of a trial - a revelation that could end his career. Detective drama, starring Lesley Sharp, Suranne Jones and Rupert Graves(n)

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: The team assists 39-year-old mother of three Miriam Aragon in her search for her Salvadoran father. After growing up in Wales with her single mother and only finding out about her father's identity at the age of 19, Miriam is desperate to find him. Meanwhile, 48-year-old Annie Sims is determined to find her birth mother, who gave her up for adoption as a baby, but kept her older sister. Following the birth of her own daughters, Annie's compassion for her biological mother has grown, and she hopes to find her and hear her side of the story. Presented by Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell(n)

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: The Barlows and Platts convene for Adam and Sarah's wedding. Tyrone and Fiz unite against Jade. Gary bumps into an old acquaintance(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Long-running soap following the ups and downs of village life amid the rolling scenery of the Yorkshire Dales(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A house-hunting couple are found dead shortly after viewing a supposedly haunted cottage. Locals claim the property is cursed - but Barnaby and Jones soon hear enough to suspect several builders, developers and estate agents of resorting to murder. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star, with George Baker, Ruth Gemmell and Jane Wymark(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Jones investigate when the body of a woman is pulled from the river during the local Oak Apple carnival. Closer inspection suggests the drowning was deliberate - and, as the detectives find out more about the villagers, they learn the killing could be linked to the death of the victim's daughter eight years previously. John Nettles and Jason Hughes play the intrepid policemen, with guest star Simon Callow(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Aircraft manufacturer Lord Mayfield hosts a party to trap a woman suspected of being a spy for the Germans. Fearing events might not go according to plan, Lady Mayfield decides to summon the famous Belgian detective to keep an eye on proceedings. Starring David Suchet, with John Stride and Phyllida Law(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A series of killings in Midsomer St Claire seem to be inspired by macabre images on a medieval fresco recently discovered in the church crypt. As the village prepares for storms and flooding, it appears that someone is using ancient torture methods to punish modern-day 'sinners'. With help from sidekick Charlie Nelson, Barnaby tries to get to the bottom of the case, and unearths a long-held secret that takes him by surprise. With Rebecca Front, Andrea Lowe, Vincent Regan and Roy Hudd(n)

programme.title: Paul O'Grady's Animal Orphans
programme.desc: Comedian Paul O'Grady presents the second of two reports from Borneo. He meets a five-year-old orang-utan that broke its arm in a fall from a tree, and is now having physiotherapy. But even if the fracture heals, will the patient have the confidence to start climbing again? The comedian also visits a centre dedicated to the conservation of sun bears - after pandas, the second most endangered bears in the world - and is introduced to a slow loris. Often sold illegally as pets, this one was so stressed it chewed off its own fingers and now needs to be taught to eat its usual diet of insects(n)

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Trans-Siberian Adventure
programme.desc: The actress reaches the midway point of her 6,000-mile journey from Hong Kong to Moscow via the Trans-Siberian Railway, taking in the varied sights of Mongolia. She spends time with native nomads, and visits a giant statue of Genghis Khan, as well as a gold mine. She also heads to the nation's capital, Ulan Bator, where she tracks down the famous Mongolian throat-singers, before catching a train bound for Russia. However, making a start on the Siberian leg of her trek proves more difficult than Joanna expects when she experiences a spot of bother at the border(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An American businessman fears his life is in danger while travelling on the Orient Express from Istanbul to Paris, and offers fellow passenger Poirot $10,000 for protection. After rejecting the proposal, the sleuth wakes up the following morning to learn the man has been stabbed to death while the train was held up by heavy snow in Serbia. Racing against time to find the killer among the passengers, Poirot must look to the past to solve the crime, and is faced with a decision that will haunt him for ever. David Suchet takes the title role in the murder mystery, with guest stars Hugh Bonneville, David Morrissey and Eileen Atkins(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Part one of two. The badly burned body of a disabled man turns up in a remote part of Manchester and a few days later another corpse is discovered. The detectives start to realise the victims were not just murdered, but tortured too. They make an arrest, but it becomes clear their investigation has just scratched the surface. Meanwhile, Janet throws her husband out and Rachel's estranged brother turns up on her doorstep. With Lisa Riley and Pippa Haywood(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Part two of two. Despite arresting four people, the detectives face a wall of silence in their investigation into the grisly murders - but they eventually make a breakthrough, which doesn't quite give them the answers they were expecting. On the domestic front, Rachel wakes up in bed with charismatic colleague Sean - a mistake she is keen never to repeat - and Janet wants to keep her newly single status a secret(n)

programme.title: The Halcyon
programme.desc: Life at the hotel continues as the Blitz rages. Emma finds Joe waiting for her at the staff entrance, remarkably curious about her work for the WVS, while Toby is worried sick after hearing a bomb fell on a shelter in Adil's neighbourhood of Paddington and killed everyone inside. Billy visits the kitchens and tells Dora that he has a job for her, while Lucian offers Lady Hamilton emotional support and Betsey and Sonny have a major altercation. Drama, starring Olivia Williams and Steven Mackintosh(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Long-running soap following the ups and downs of village life amid the rolling scenery of the Yorkshire Dales(n)

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Curly clings to the hope that Raquel has got last-minute nerves. Ken brings Denise and Daniel home. Curly calls Des out of the betting shop for a fight(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Raquel is distressed to hear Des and Curly have been fighting, and Bet calls on Curly when Raquel fears he might be suicidal. Denise is irritated by Ken not leaving her alone(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jack tries to apologise to Sarah, Zoe has an alternative proposal for Emma. Robert goes missing when Jack confronts Andrew about having an affair with Sarah(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jack frantically searches for Robert. Emma eventually agrees to move in with Zoe in Emmerdale. Rachel tells Chris about her relationship insecurities(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Lord Ashfordly's tenants are angered when his new estate manager Jimbo Brady employs the hard-line methods learned in South Africa and raises their rents. Brian Rogers faces eviction, but suspicion is more widespread when Jimbo's vehicle is sabotaged. Meanwhile, Peggy believes she has found the explanation for David's recent behaviour(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy and Jo are seeking an additional female flatmate, so Robin has to get in touch with his feminine side. The first episode of the 1970s comedy series, with Richard O'Sullivan, Paula Wilcox and Sally Thomsett(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: The housemates are put out when Chrissy's mother arrives for an unscheduled visit. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Feature-length episode of the crime drama. DI Frost has his hands full coping with death threats, arson and a staff shortage at the station - but anything is better than staying at home with an unwelcome guest. David Jason stars as the grouchy inspector, with Tony Haygarth, Diane Bull, Denise Black and Dominic Jephcott(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Gina and Phil make the final preparations for their big day, but the groom's uneventful stag night stirs up some pre-wedding nerves. Rob and Peggy prepare a surprise for the soon-to-be-wed couple, and the village grows concerned when an elderly resident mysteriously disappears. Tricia Penrose, Mark Jordon, Jonathan Kerrigan and Gwen Taylor star(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The search for Robert continues in daylight. Betty is appalled by the mess at the McAllisters. The police suspect Jack might have something to do with his son's disappearance(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jack is still convinced that Sarah has been having an affair. Tina warns Luke that her brothers will react badly to the news that they spent the night together(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Des is dismayed to hear Curly has lost his job. Nick Wilding hands over Vicky's trust fund of 240,000 pounds. Bet warns Vicky that men might be attracted to her for her money(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ken feels a bit old for his fatherly duties. Sean celebrates his divorce being finalised. Don drives a woman around Manchester to help her find her daughter(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A Midsomer Malham resident is accused of battering her best friend to death, but Barnaby cannot help feeling the evidence against her does not add up. The detective enlists the help of a pensioner, who also happens to be one of the key witnesses, to find out what really happened on the night of the murder - and realises the elderly lady's life could be at risk. With John Nettles, Jason Hughes and Thelma Barlow(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Gina and Phil make the final preparations for their big day, but the groom's uneventful stag night stirs up some pre-wedding nerves. Rob and Peggy prepare a surprise for the soon-to-be-wed couple, and the village grows concerned when an elderly resident mysteriously disappears. Tricia Penrose, Mark Jordon, Jonathan Kerrigan and Gwen Taylor star(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective is summoned to a hotel by a mysterious veiled lady who tells him that her forthcoming marriage is under threat because she is being blackmailed over a love letter. Drama, starring David Suchet and Frances Barber(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: As England and Argentina kick off in the World Cup quarter-final, Oxford's streets are deserted. At an almost empty museum, Adrian Weiss, a specialist in heraldry and genealogy, is found dead - slain by a ceremonial dagger with links to 19th-century India. An entry in the visitors' book leads Morse to a girls' boarding school, where a note reading 'Save Me' is mysteriously slipped into his coat pocket. Digging into the building's troubled history, he finds a connection between Weiss's untimely fate and a series of unsolved murders that occurred on the school's grounds almost 100 years earlier. 1960s-set drama, starring Shaun Evans, with Roger Allam, Anton Lesser, James Bradshaw and Abigail Thaw(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Janet comes face to face with the man who left her fighting for life just months earlier - serial killer Geoff Hastings, who has vowed to finally reveal the full extent of his crimes. Rachel and the rest of the team investigate the racially motivated murder of a taxi driver. On the personal front, Andy refuses to take no for an answer, so Janet threatens to report him to the DCI. Crime drama, starring Lesley Sharp and Suranne Jones, with an appearance by Kevin Doyle (Downton Abbey's Molesley)(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Feature-length episode of the crime drama. DI Frost has his hands full coping with death threats, arson and a staff shortage at the station - but anything is better than staying at home with an unwelcome guest. programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Raquel is distressed to hear Des and Curly have been fighting, and Bet calls on Curly when Raquel fears he might be suicidal. Denise is irritated by Ken not leaving her alone(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jack tries to apologise to Sarah, Zoe has an alternative proposal for Emma. Robert goes missing when Jack confronts Andrew about having an affair with Sarah(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jack frantically searches for Robert. Emma eventually agrees to move in with Zoe in Emmerdale. Rachel tells Chris about her relationship insecurities(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Lord Ashfordly's tenants are angered when his new estate manager Jimbo Brady employs the hard-line methods learned in South Africa and raises their rents. Brian Rogers faces eviction, but suspicion is more widespread when Jimbo's vehicle is sabotaged. Meanwhile, Peggy believes she has found the explanation for David's recent behaviour(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy and Jo are seeking an additional female flatmate, so Robin has to get in touch with his feminine side. The first episode of the 1970s comedy series, with Richard O'Sullivan, Paula Wilcox and Sally Thomsett(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: The housemates are put out when Chrissy's mother arrives for an unscheduled visit. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Feature-length episode of the crime drama. DI Frost has his hands full coping with death threats, arson and a staff shortage at the station - but anything is better than staying at home with an unwelcome guest. David Jason stars as the grouchy inspector, with Tony Haygarth, Diane Bull, Denise Black and Dominic Jephcott(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Gina and Phil make the final preparations for their big day, but the groom's uneventful stag night stirs up some pre-wedding nerves. Rob and Peggy prepare a surprise for the soon-to-be-wed couple, and the village grows concerned when an elderly resident mysteriously disappears. Tricia Penrose, Mark Jordon, Jonathan Kerrigan and Gwen Taylor star(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The search for Robert continues in daylight. Betty is appalled by the mess at the McAllisters. The police suspect Jack might have something to do with his son's disappearance(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jack is still convinced that Sarah has been having an affair. Tina warns Luke that her brothers will react badly to the news that they spent the night together(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Des is dismayed to hear Curly has lost his job. Nick Wilding hands over Vicky's trust fund of 240,000 pounds. Bet warns Vicky that men might be attracted to her for her money(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ken feels a bit old for his fatherly duties. Sean celebrates his divorce being finalised. Don drives a woman around Manchester to help her find her daughter(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A Midsomer Malham resident is accused of battering her best friend to death, but Barnaby cannot help feeling the evidence against her does not add up. The detective enlists the help of a pensioner, who also happens to be one of the key witnesses, to find out what really happened on the night of the murder - and realises the elderly lady's life could be at risk. With John Nettles, Jason Hughes and Thelma Barlow(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Gina and Phil make the final preparations for their big day, but the groom's uneventful stag night stirs up some pre-wedding nerves. Rob and Peggy prepare a surprise for the soon-to-be-wed couple, and the village grows concerned when an elderly resident mysteriously disappears. Tricia Penrose, Mark Jordon, Jonathan Kerrigan and Gwen Taylor star(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective is summoned to a hotel by a mysterious veiled lady who tells him that her forthcoming marriage is under threat because she is being blackmailed over a love letter. Drama, starring David Suchet and Frances Barber(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: As England and Argentina kick off in the World Cup quarter-final, Oxford's streets are deserted. At an almost empty museum, Adrian Weiss, a specialist in heraldry and genealogy, is found dead - slain by a ceremonial dagger with links to 19th-century India. An entry in the visitors' book leads Morse to a girls' boarding school, where a note reading 'Save Me' is mysteriously slipped into his coat pocket. Digging into the building's troubled history, he finds a connection between Weiss's untimely fate and a series of unsolved murders that occurred on the school's grounds almost 100 years earlier. 1960s-set drama, starring Shaun Evans, with Roger Allam, Anton Lesser, James Bradshaw and Abigail Thaw(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Janet comes face to face with the man who left her fighting for life just months earlier - serial killer Geoff Hastings, who has vowed to finally reveal the full extent of his crimes. Rachel and the rest of the team investigate the racially motivated murder of a taxi driver. On the personal front, Andy refuses to take no for an answer, so Janet threatens to report him to the DCI. Crime drama, starring Lesley Sharp and Suranne Jones, with an appearance by Kevin Doyle (Downton Abbey's Molesley)(n)

programme.title: The Professionals

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A trusted informant tips Regan off about an upcoming robbery, but the gang involved kidnaps his daughter to blackmail him into taking no action. Police drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: Live Darts: UK Open
programme.desc: Jacqui Oatley presents coverage of the evening session on day two at the Butlin's Minehead Resort, as the event nicknamed the FA Cup of Darts - due to the random nature of the draw - continues with sixth-round matches. Eventual champion Nathan Aspinall recorded a victory over Steve Lennon at this stage last year, while Dave Chisnall, James Wade and Simon Whitlock all suffered shock defeats in round six, losing to Simon Stevenson, Ross Smith and Josh Payne respectively. However, wins for Rob Cross, Michael Smith and Gerwyn Price ensured the quarter-finals did not have a wholly unfamiliar feel. With analysis from Alan Warriner-Little, Chris Mason and Mark Webster, and commentary by Stuart Pyke, John Rawling and Dan Dawson(n)

programme.title: Assassins (1995)
programme.desc: Hitman Robert Rath is planning to quit his lethal profession, especially when he is stalked by a ruthless younger assassin who has no qualms about killing innocent bystanders. But as Rath takes on his last assignment, he decides to protect his intended target after his rival comes gunning for her. Thriller, starring Sylvester Stallone, Antonio Banderas and Julianne Moore(n)

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: A report from Southeast Asia, of something in the water that's sliced cleanly through a young man's testicles, is enough to lure 'fishing detective' Jeremy Wade to the shores of the Mekong - a river long renowned for its monstrous fish. But what kind of creature could have inflicted such a distinctive, surgical wound? In his quest to expose the unseen mutilator, Jeremy uncovers multiple victims who've been mauled, half-blinded, and even fatally wounded by the same mysterious assailant - a baby-faced killer that appears to be proliferating(n)

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade returns to Africa's Okavango Delta to reinvestigate the tigerfish - a pack-hunting carnivore previously blamed for mauling the victims of a capsized boat. If these fish are as lethal as Jeremy concluded, then why aren't there more reports of fatal, piranha-like attacks? In a bid to flush out the true nature of these sight-hunting predators, Jeremy puts on his dive gear and offers himself as live bait(n)

programme.title: Live Darts: UK Open
programme.desc: Jacqui Oatley presents coverage of the evening session on day three at the Butlin's Minehead Resort, as the event nicknamed the FA Cup of Darts concludes with the semi-finals and final. Nathan Aspinall and Rob Cross prevailed over Gerwyn Price and Michael Smith respectively in the last four 12 months ago, before Aspinall prevailed 11-5 in the showpiece match, recording a 170 checkout in the final leg to win his maiden PDC title. With analysis from Alan Warriner-Little, Chris Mason and Mark Webster, commentary by Stuart Pyke and John Rawling, and reports from Dan Dawson(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: An antiques shop is used as a point of access in a bank robbery, and it looks like an inside job, but Regan suspects otherwise and soon finds himself on a collision course with Haskins. Police drama, starring John Thaw, Dennis Waterman and Garfield Morgan(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur loses a fortune in cash when his wine supplier is mugged and the money he entrusted is stolen. To add insult to injury the dodgy entrepreneur becomes the prime suspect for the attack, leading him and Terry on a mission to prove his innocence. George Cole stars(n)

programme.title: Only When I Laugh
programme.desc: A new patient is admitted to the ward, and an atmosphere of resentment develops when he is allocated the coveted bed by the window. The first episode of the hospital sitcom, starring James Bolam, Christopher Strauli and Peter Bowles(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: The suspicious death of a Holocaust survivor presents Quincy with a fresh mystery to solve, and sets him on the trail of a Nazi war criminal determined to evade capture(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: A gypsy tries to thwart Theo's pursuit of the serial killer who murdered several members of her clan, as she intends to carry out her own lethal brand of vengeance(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon Templar is suspected of involvement in a series of jewel robberies on the French Riviera, and has to stay one step ahead of the police long enough to catch the real culprit. Guest starring Ronnie Barker and Aimee Delamain(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Terry gives shelter to a friend who has broken out of prison, but his act of charity backfires, costing poor old Arthur dearly. Derek Thompson (Casualty) and Sherrie Hewson (Loose Women) feature alongside George Cole and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle are assigned to protect a deposed African politician, who is due to attend a conference with a view to regaining power. After foiling two assassination attempts, they soon appreciate others share information on his whereabouts. Charles Dance and Geoffrey Palmer feature alongside stars Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: The Flying Squad foils the hijacking of a security truck before attempting to learn the identity of an informer. Colin Welland guest stars in the hard-hitting police drama, alongside John Thaw and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: The medical examiner comes to the aid of an agoraphobic woman when she claims to have witnessed a murder, even though there is no sign of a body(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: A woman is the only witness to the murder of a police officer, but her gangster boyfriend is an old enemy of Theo, and tries to intimidate her into keeping quiet(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon Templar encounters a girl who claims to be Hitler's daughter, and helps her evade Neo-Nazis. Vintage adventure, starring Roger Moore(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur sees an opportunity to cash in when a footballer with a drink and gambling habit goes missing, but selling his story to the paper is not as easy as it seems when there is more than one person interested in his whereabouts. Comedy drama, with George Cole and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle suspect Russian intervention when a mentally unstable lieutenant colonel holding valuable Nato secrets disappears from an army psychiatric hospital. Lewis Collins, Martin Shaw and Gordon Jackson star(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: With Haskins in Toronto, his stand-in DCI Quirk (guest star Bill Maynard) becomes obsessed with catching a gang of thieves. When information is received suggesting the crooks are about to rob a jeweller's shop, officers are poised for action, but events take an unexpected turn as the informant seemingly has a score to settle with Quirk first. Hard-hitting police drama, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: Cycling: Paris-Nice Highlights
programme.desc: Gary Imlach presents action from stage two, featuring a 166.5km course from Chevreuse to Chalette-sur-Loing. With commentary by Ned Boulting and David Millar, and reports from Daniel Friebe(n)

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: On a volcanic island in the Pacific Ring of Fire, an ancient fishing community is being terrorised by something in the water that has left behind a trail of bloodied victims. With a collective knowledge built up over centuries, these fishermen thought they knew everything in these waters - until now. The only clue they have to the attacker's identity are the large puncture wounds it has left behind. Here, angling detective Jeremy Wade journeys to this land of fire and folklore to put himself to the ultimate test - can he uncover the identity of this mysterious fanged assailant?(n)

programme.title: Giant Lobster Hunters
programme.desc: With the winter lobster season in full swing, Squizzy, the skipper of Bold Contender, is under pressure to fill a special order of 600 brindle lobsters. The engine of Ansons Bay stops working, and stormy weather and rough seas plague the Minnamurra, while Danny, the captain of William Norling, receives some good news when hunting for big reds in the Bass Strait(n)

programme.title: Magnum Force (1973)
programme.desc: Maverick cop Dirty Harry Callahan antagonises his superiors once more in his pursuit of a vigilante group responsible for murdering the city's most wanted criminals. However, he is unaware that the killers are some of his fellow officers - and are prepared to see him dead to keep their secret safe. Action thriller sequel, starring Clint Eastwood, Hal Holbrook, Mitchell Ryan, David Soul and Robert Urich(n)

programme.title: Royal Doctors: Treating the Monarchy

The programme looks at the tricks the royals use to avoid gorging at banquets, reveals how Prince Philip can still fit in his marriage uniform and what it is really like to give birth inside the Lindo Wing. Plus, historian Kate Williams explores some of the gruesome tales from royal history(n)

programme.title: Charles and Di: The Truth Behind Their Wedding

programme.title: The Carpenters: Their Greatest Hits

Each is accompanied by interesting facts about the act President Richard Nixon once described as 'Young America at its very best'(n)

programme.title: Autopsy

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords

programme.desc: HMS Duncan and her crew face increasing pressure in the Strait of Hormuz as Iran steps up its efforts to capture a second British tanker. Three weeks into Duncan's mission, Iran is still holding the Stena Impero captive, but international pressure is mounting against them. The ship is ordered to hold the line and continue escorting fuel tankers safely through the Strait, while awaiting relief by HMS Kent. When a fire breaks out in the engine room, it threatens the ship and the lives of some of the crew(n)

programme.title: Great British Ships
programme.desc: Rob Bell reveals the secrets of the historic ships that shaped the nation. He begins with the story of HMS Victory, Admiral Nelson's personal flagship at the Battle of Trafalgar. When Victory was built more than 40 years previously, she was the largest warship in the world with firepower equivalent to a whole army on land. However, Rob discovers that this titan of the seas was very nearly scrapped long before the battle that would ultimately seal her place in history(n)

programme.title: Con Air (1997)

When they hijack the plane in mid-flight, led by criminal mastermind Cyrus the Virus, Poe is forced to intervene. Meanwhile, US marshal Vince Larkin has an ongoing battle on the ground to prevent the plane from being blown to pieces. Director Simon West's blockbusting action thriller, starring Nicolas Cage, John Cusack, John Malkovich, Steve Buscemi, Ving Rhames and Colm Meaney(n)

Comedy sequel, starring Peter Sellers, Herbert Lom, Lesley-Anne Down, Burt Kwouk and Leonard Rossiter(n)

Drama, starring Chris Evans, Lindsay Duncan and Mckenna Grace(n)

Action thriller sequel, with Bruce Willis, Timothy Olyphant, Justin Long and Maggie Q(n)

Sam Raimi's horror, starring Alison Lohman, Justin Long and Lorna Raver(n)

A 40-year-old slacker realises what is going on, and hatches a scheme to go into business as a zombie slayer, laying the dead to rest for a price. Comedy horror, starring Alexis Diaz de Villegas and Jorge Molina. In Spanish(n)

Sci-fi adventure based on Jules Verne's novel, starring Pat Boone, James Mason, Arlene Dahl and Diane Baker(n)

As chaos erupts around the world, experts hatch a plan to halt the destruction and save the human race - but it involves venturing deep below the planet's surface to detonate a nuclear bomb. Sci-fi thriller, starring Aaron Eckhart, Hilary Swank, Stanley Tucci and DJ Qualls(n)

programme.title: Suffragette Interview Special

Comedy, with Parminder Nagra, Keira Knightley, Jonathan Rhys Meyers, Juliet Stevenson and Shaznay Lewis(n)

Drama, starring Patricia Roc, Eric Portman, Gordon Jackson and Basil Radford(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Fitness instructor Tanya Manock offers up a Mediterranean-themed menu as she kicks off a week of dinner parties at her home in Rochdale, Greater Manchester, where one of her guests - medium Dean Fox - stumbles upon a plethora of lingerie in the bedroom(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Food-lover Rod Tonge hosts the second dinner party in Rochdale, Greater Manchester, hoping his efforts will win him the £1,000 cash prize. However, his chances are low as he has already insulted fitness instructor Tanya Manock, and HR manager Claire Lemon does not have high hopes for his entertaining skills(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: HR manager Claire Lemon hopes to compensate for her 'imperfect cooking skills' with her bubbly personality and superior hosting ability as she hosts on day three of the dinner-party challenge in Rochdale, Greater Manchester. But tension rises when Tanya barely touches the starter - or the main course(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Psychic medium Dean Fox puts his cooking skills to the test during the fourth dinner party in Rochdale, Greater Manchester. His main course goes down well with his fellow diners, but his pudding jeopardises his bid to pick up the £1,000 prize(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's the last night in Rochdale, Greater Manchester, and the turn of feisty mum Sadiya Hussein to host. The tension between Sadiya and Tanya threatens to reach boiling point, and the evening takes a curious turn when Sadiya decides to put psychic medium Dean Fox's clairvoyant powers to the test(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at David and Ewa Woodward's Keats Cottage in Shanklin on the Isle of Wight. In the rooms, Donna Taylor from Jasmine House in Leicestershire makes a hair-raising discovery, while mother-and-daughter team Lesley and Chloe Priestly take a dislike to one of their competitors at dinner(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Jasmine House in Lutterworth, Leicestershire managed by Donna Taylor who believes in a no frills approach. But it doesn't take long for Martin and Sara to realise all the added extras in the room don't add up. During the afternoon Leslie and Chloe show their fighting spirit during a kickboxing lesson and all the B&Bers square up to their host(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to the luxury Halliwell House in Cheshire for the third day of the contest, where owners Martin Hulme and Sara Williams charge nearly £500 per night. Donna is astonished at the room rates, while David and Ewa are expecting nothing but perfection, but the self-service approach at breakfast confuses the guests, leaving Martin and Sara defiant in the face of their feedback(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final two days see the contestants staying the night at Soar Chapel Guest House in Ebbw Vale, the family home of mum and daughter Lesley and Chloe Priestly. Expectations are high for this duo and one guest in particular is not disappointed. Things get going in the afternoon during a beatboxing contest, although old tensions resurface at dinner and the hosts are left to calm things down. A massive spread is laid on at breakfast, but when one guest takes issue with the cooked items, Lesley and Chloe worry what impact it will have come payment day(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. David and Ewa Woodward want to challenge Martin's criticism over the way they run their business. Next, Donna Taylor wants to find out more about the issue that caused Martin and Sara to lose sleep. Needless to say, there is tension in the air and axes to grind, before the winner is revealed(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Make-up artist Jules Osmany hosts the first of the week's dinner parties in Northamptonshire, and hopes her Indian-Moroccan fusion menu and laid-back attitude will win her the £1,000 prize. Joining her are tomboy Lauren Oxlade, stay-at-home mum Stephanie Reeder, MD Carl Johnson and funeral service manager Peter Pickering(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Funeral service manager Peter Pickering hosts the second dinner party in Northamptonshire, hoping his traditional approach will win him the £1,000 cash prize. However, one guest does not like bread and butter pudding, and Stephanie is horrified by Jules's shocking revelation(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: As the third party from Northamptonshire gets under way, stay-at-home mum Stephanie Reeder's nerves are evident as she plans to serve classic fish and chips, while offering a glamorous clothes-swapping session for entertainment(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Business planning manager Lauren Oxlade is the penultimate host in Northamptonshire. She reveals herself to be the dark horse of the competition when she invites a few surprising friends along to the party, and impresses with her organisational skills and passionate cooking style(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Last to try his hand in Northamptonshire is managing director and proud northerner Carl Johnson, with a menu that includes gammon cooked in two litres of cola. Stephanie and Lauren are treated to a glimpse of the host in all his glory when they see what he has hanging above his bed - but it remains to be seen what the guests will make of his lumpy chocolate mousse(n)

Who will be leaving the competition and which three potters will go through to the grand finale?(n)

programme.desc: In Yorkshire, a man requires urgent medical care after falling from his motorbike. The medics scramble to the Lake District when a walker goes into a cardiac arrest on Ullswater(n)

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E

It's the second time she has fallen in two months and medics want to find out if there's a more serious underlying cause. Meanwhile, the team keeps a close eye on 75-year-old Bob, who has had a suspected heart attack, and Ben, 25, isn't overly concerned by his swollen finger, until a doctor diagnoses cellulitis(n)

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: Documentary following the emergency services in Wiltshire, continuing with crimes relating to the rising use of cannabis. PC Joe Tompkinson is cut up at a roundabout and when he stops the car responsible, all he can smell is the drug. PC Adam Burson visits a family whose members have had a brick thrown through their front window - a dealer's retaliation - and officers are called by a mother after she is assaulted by her son, who is suspected to be suffering from cannabis psychosis(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: At the Bunkroom hostel in Chester, David Sharrock is determined to prove that hostelling is for everybody - even if his wife Victoria isn't convinced. programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Giant otter couple Diego and Icana are the best of friends but have never mated, so keepers hope a new male will have what it takes to relight Icana's fire. Blue morpho butterflies Macuna and Peanut also need to mate, but when their diet of fruit ferments and turns to alcohol, their drunken behaviour might dash Chester's hopes of producing the next generation of butterflies. Cameras also follow the relationship between mum-and-daughter Andrean bears Lima and Madidi, who are fighting over food and personal space. Can the youngster learn to respect her elder?(n)

programme.desc: Dick and Angel as they host a very special wedding for close friends. This time, they get down and dirty in an off-road scramble versus the Bentley Bentayga - can a car synonymous with class and style be beaten by cars made from cast-offs?(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and Richard Hammond debate which company has made the greatest number of exceptional vehicles, and James May finds a way of transporting caravans to campsites without inconveniencing other road users. The Top Gear track plays host to a replica of one of the world's most famous rally cars, and broadcaster Chris Evans - some five years before he was announced as the show's new presenter - is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson puts the Renaultsport Twingo 133 through a stringent examination in Belfast - with a helping hand from actor Ross Kemp. Richard Hammond samples the delights of racing airport vehicles, and James May takes the Vauxhall Insignia VXR for a drive in the company of Margaret Calvert, whose ideas were central to the design of modern British road signs. Film director Guy Ritchie is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car, and there is also a group test of high-performance off-roaders from BMW, Audi and Range Rover(n)

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: At the halfway stage, all contestants - except Phil Wang - are in with a chance of victory. Kerry Godliman and Jessica Knappett resort to soapy violence(n)

programme.title: Room 101
programme.desc: Frank Skinner invites actor Ross Kemp, singer Sophie Ellis-Bextor and comedian Rob Beckett to vent their spleen about people and objects that upset them, as the trio vie to banish their top peeves, annoyances, irritation or worst nightmares to the depths of Room 101 for ever. Their gripes include misplaced clapping, teeth, expensive water in hotel rooms and snobby shop assistants(n)

programme.title: Room 101
programme.desc: Frank Skinner invites Kaiser Chiefs' frontman Ricky Wilson, and comedians Joe Lycett and Zoe Lyons to express their anger over people and objects that upset them, as the trio vie to banish their top peeves, annoyances, irritation or worst nightmares consigned to oblivion. Their gripes include new towels, escalators and the bartender at the local pub(n)

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Doc Martin star Martin Clunes takes over the reins for extended edition of the satirical quiz, with comedian Jon Richardson and Newsnight presenter Kirsty Wark joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the stories making the headlines(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Host Stephen Fry poses tough questions about Happiness to Andy Hamilton, Rich Hall, Phill Jupitus and Alan Davies, and awards points for the answers he deems most interesting(n)

programme.title: Room 101
programme.desc: Frank Skinner invites comedians Lucy Porter and Romesh Ranganathan and TV judge Robert Rinder to express their anger over people, events and objects that upset them, as the trio vie to banish their top peeves, annoyances, irritation or worst nightmares consigned to oblivion. Their gripes include lavish proposals, school reunions and New Year's Eve(n)

programme.title: Meet the Richardsons
programme.desc: Comedy series starring real-life married couple Jon Richardson and Lucy Beaumont as they offer a fictional view of their life together. Jon is given career advice from his comedy hero, Jack Dee. Meanwhile, Lucy signs Jon up to be the celebrity judge at the World Dock Pudding Competition in Hebden Bridge(n)

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Guests Miles Jupp, Ava Vidal and Carl Donnelly join Hugh Dennis, Andy Parsons and Chris Addison for a 2011 edition of the topical comedy quiz show. Dara O Briain hosts(n)

programme.title: Secrets of the Bible
programme.desc: Documentary offering an insight into the staff of Moses, one of the more astonishing miracles depicted in the Bible as Moses turned his staff into a snake(n)

programme.title: The Diary of Anne Frank: A Tale of Two Sisters
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the story of Anne Frank and Eva Schloss, whose lives intertwined during the darkest of period of 20th-century history. Eva, who met Anne in Amsterdam in 1938, survived Auschwitz and here talks about her memories of the young diarist, her survival from the death camps, and how she became the step daughter of Anne's Father, Otto Frank(n)

programme.title: Secret Nazi Bases
programme.desc: One of the largest Nazi bunkers ever constructed, outside the French town of Watten, a missile facility with storage for 108 missiles, a liquid oxygen factory and two launch pads(n)

programme.title: Deep Wreck Mysteries
programme.desc: An investigation into a wreck in the Bristol Channel identified by divers as a First World War hospital ship, the sinking of which constituted a major war crime(n)

programme.title: Deep Wreck Mysteries
programme.desc: Divers explore the wreck of First World War battleship HMS Audacious, which was once the pride of the Royal Navy and thought to be 'unsinkable' until a single unexpected blow sealed its fate 14 miles off the coast of Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Deep Wreck Mysteries
programme.desc: Part one of two. Using the latest diving technology to explore the South-West's deep-sea wrecks(n)

programme.title: Deep Wreck Mysteries
programme.desc: Experts look into new areas of technology to locate the SS Armenian, a cargo liner carrying 1,400 mules, which was sunk by a German U-Boat in 1915 but never found(n)

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: The naturalist ventures into uncharted territory in pursuit of new discoveries. He takes on physical challenges, encounters extraordinary wildlife and meets remarkable people. In the first edition, Steve and a team of climbers and jungle survival experts head into the ancient landscape of South America's Guiana Shield. This vast wilderness of pristine forests and towering table-top mountains is one of the most inaccessible terrains on the planet(n)

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: In the Jebel Akhdar mountain range of Oman, Steve leads an expedition into an unexplored desert canyon. With temperatures soaring, dehydration and heat stroke are a constant threat(n)

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve and a team of kayakers head to the Himalayas to visit the last great unexplored river in Bhutan. Legend has it that at some point, the river drops off the face of the planet(n)

programme.title: Big in America: British Hits in the USA
programme.desc: Compilation of British rock 'n' roll acts in performance, featuring songs that crossed over to the US charts. From the Dave Clark Five to Coldplay, the Brits have rocked America and sometimes even done better across the pond than on home soil. A Flock of Seagulls, Supertramp and Bush also feature, alongside darker British global exports like Black Sabbath and the Cure(n)

programme.title: Duran Duran: There's Something You Should Know
programme.desc: Simon Le Bon, John Taylor, Nick Rhodes and Roger Taylor open up about the highs and lows of the band's career - with archive footage of former guitarist Andy Taylor helping round out the story. The documentary charts their trajectory through seven of their 14 albums, each one uncovering a compelling chapter in the history of five lads from Birmingham who had one simple dream - to become the biggest band in the world. Contributors include Boy George, Nile Rodgers and Mark Ronson(n)

programme.title: The True Story of the Mary Celeste
programme.desc: On December 4, 1872, the Mary Celeste was found adrift in the Atlantic with its cargo fully intact, yet with no one on board. Experts use CGI and fresh evidence in a bid to solve the mystery of the ship and learn how and why its crew disappeared(n)

programme.title: World's Greatest Palaces
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of Lukshmi Villas Palace in India, which was built for the Maharaja of Baroda in 1890 and remains the home of the royal family to this day(n)

programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Chris attempts to cross the length of Cuba, the only island in the Caribbean to boast an extensive railway network, starting in Havana in the west. This proves to be a major challenge given the unreliability of the service and the old trains regularly used, but makes for a fascinating journey to Chris's ultimate goal, the American naval base at Guantanamo Bay. Along the way he soaks up the sights and sounds of Havana, retraces the footsteps of the country's national hero Che Guevara, and visits the ghost town of Camilo Cienfuegos - a relic from Cuba's colonial sugar plantation era(n)

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: It was 30 years after the Romans invaded Britain that they were ready to take on the challenge of conquering the Lake District. With the toughest landscape they had encountered in the nation, peopled by a rebellious tribe, it was no small task, but within a few years, the whole of Lakeland was under their control. Tony Robinson tackles the journey by himself, walking 50 miles from Penrith to Ambleside and on to the Irish Sea at Ravenglass, discovering that the Romans encountered beauty and danger in equal measure(n)

programme.title: First Dates

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: In this special episode, the High Court enforcement agents have to deal with a fraudulent tenant, an evasive debtor and a mother in denial. Steve and Ben are in west London to see a tenant who owes £14,000 in unpaid rent, but when they gain access to the property they discover the house has divided up like a hotel and is being sublet. In Sussex, Brian and Del chase a debt of over £10,000 owed to a van rental business for damaged vehicles, before heading to Kent to try to reclaim £6,000 of unpaid rent from a woman financially dependent on a new partner after a disastrous divorce(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: In south London, father-and-son team Del and Dael Anglin face death threats from a debtor's father, after arriving at a property to collect a debt of over £3,500. Meanwhile, in east London, two agents are confronted with a tenant who claims to have had no prior warning that he is being evicted from his tiny bedsit. Del and Brian try to recover £6,000 owed by a London hotel to a catering supply company, and Paul and Steve must tackle a difficult eviction(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: In north London, agents Del and Dael chase nearly £2,000 owed by a printing company, but the owner denies the debt and starts getting physical. Meanwhile, Stewart and Elmor find themselves caught up in a personal dispute when they try to collect rent arrears from a barber's shop, and in Bedfordshire, Del and Brian try to recover almost £2,000 owed to a funeral director. Stewart and Vic track down a debt of £5,000 from the owner of a computer repair shop in Lincolnshire(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Stewart and Elmor visit Nottingham to chase almost £8,000 owed by a car dealership to a dissatisfied customer, while on the Isle of Wight, Brian and Del try to collect £3,700 of unpaid estate agency fees. programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

programme.title: Law & Order

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: An Iraqi immigrant kills a female veteran of the second Gulf War for torturing her brother at Abu Ghraib prison. Starring Jesse L Martin and Dennis Farina(n)

programme.title: Law & Order

programme.title: Monkey Life

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Wildlife vet John Lewis provides a health check for stump-tailed macaques who have recently arrived from a lab facility in Scotland, but discovers they have serious dental problems. Meanwhile, ring-tailed lemur Kaiyah has her first taste of life in the trees(n)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Baby golden-cheeked gibbon Mikado ventures outside for the first time, while animal director Jeremy tries to coax overweight Oshine to the outdoor enclosure - but to get there, the reluctant orang-utan must first navigate a series of tunnels. Elsewhere, chimp Lulu refuses to take her medicine(n)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: The team travels to Lebanon on a rescue mission to collect lonely chimp Kiki, who has spent the past eight years in a cage that has now rusted to the point where it cannot be opened. Meanwhile, in Dorset, overweight orang-utan Oshine proves to be a surprise star pupil(n)

programme.title: Monkey Life

programme.title: Monkey Life

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Animal director Jeremy decides to find out how much weight orang-utan Oshine has lost since she arrived at the park, chimp Hananya is taken by surprise when he meets Kiki, and baby golden-cheeked gibbon Mikado shows off the latest stages of his colour change(n)

programme.title: Motorway Patrol
programme.desc: Documentary following members of the New Zealand highway patrol as they witness some of the best and worst behaviour on the nation's motorways. The series focuses on Auckland, the country's largest urban area, and features motoring chaos including major traffic collisions, escaped animals and pedestrians on busy roads. There is also footage of drivers falling asleep at the wheel, overloaded vehicles and suspects caught with illegal substances - plus a host of excuses as the people pulled over try to persuade officers they have not done anything wrong(n)

programme.desc: The doctors reveal the secrets to sticking to health resolutions made at New Year, with surgeon Gabriel Weston experimenting with a herbal supplement said to help people drink less alcohol. Dr Chris van Tulleken reveals unusual ways of getting a better night's sleep, and Michael Mosley discovers how to stay motivated. programme.desc: A group of courageous Italian nuns risks everything during the Second World War. programme.title: Upstairs Downstairs
programme.desc: Lady Persie's life has become increasingly complex, as she continues her illicit affair and pursues her right-wing political interests with little regard for the potential danger to those she loves. Meanwhile, Lady Agnes prepares for motherhood by having her picture taken by celebrated photographer Cecil Beaton - a visit that causes excitement for one particular member of the downstairs staff - and Sir Hallam finds himself dragged into the crisis surrounding the abdication of Edward VIII facing the British monarchy. Keeley Hawes, Ed Stoppard, Anne Reid and Claire Foy star in the period drama(n)

programme.title: The Bionic Vet
